I went to the dealership to get a refund for the unused warranty on my car I recently traded in and after went to look at the cars. Some employee comes up to me and tells me "sorry these cars are for customers only". Um didn't it occure to you that people can own more than one car. Or maybe we traded in our last van to make room for 2 new smaller cars. He shouldn't assume before he knew the facts. Since this is the type of people representing the company, I'm never considered a Honda again. Then for the year after I keep getting a total of 6 letters (month apart) telling me my warranty is about to expire for the same car I traded in and got the warranty canceled a year back.

Originally gave three stars and they wanted to know what they can do to make it five.
I'm changing my rating to give because maybe I wasn't fair.
International Market Place has upgraded themselves many times over. It is a stellar example of an open air center, as compared to modern enclosed shopping areas.
Coming to Hawaii is our climate. Not some enclosed air conditioned building.
It is this same reason for the original rating of three stars. I wanted to see Hawaiian stuff. Yeah the tourist trap thing. Tiki, hula, lush tropical stuff. No Shave Ice, Tutus in muumuu, beachboys, locals spreading aloha spirit. Place should smell of flowers, not perfume, exotic air fresheners and coconut soap. I hardly saw any Hawaii stuff. Even the fountains didn't say Hawaii.  It said civilization. Granted it was better than walking on Kalakaua. More better Stay in California and walk Rodeo Drive.
The Center itself is a beautiful show case of   what a shopping area can be. I can't suggest any improvements.

The Basalt Mai Tai was also super delicious. Service was excellent. Enjoyed every part of the meal. Will be back to try breakfast because it looks awesome.

This Costco is like the plucky younger sibling of the couple giant stores in Honolulu and Hawaii Kai. Parking is good, lines - even went on a Sunday - weren’t bad, and you can actually maneuver the aisles without feeling like a salmon running downstream. While their selection isn’t quite as wide or selective, I trust you’ll find everything required for the family picnic at nearby White Plains Beach!

Locally owned. Authentic feel. Longtime pillar in business community. I really want to love this place but think it is alright. Big part of this is their service. Strikes me as warm toward long-time regulars but lukewarm-to-indifferent towards haole transplants like me. Bakery-wise we got an amazing cake from them but find their donuts respectable but not memorable. A better choice than Safeway but not worth a trip unto itself (except for cake, yum! Bumps them up a star)
Clean and overpriced. Like every Best Buy. Lol

One of my favorite poke places on the island. Their fried chicken & fish patties balance out their poke, so if you're craving a plate lunch, lots to choose from. Definitely a stop by for tourists.

It tough to find some decent Mexican food in Hawaii. I lived in Texas for a couple of years, experienced a ton of great, authentic Mexican food. This place makes great food. Their street tacos, with all of the different choices of meats, are by far my favorite. They also have a salsa bar, help yourself to some awesome sides. This is the place to go for your Taco Tuesday.

Blueberry Cream Cheese Scones. That's all you need. Buy a dozen, share them with your family for breakfast or brunch or post-hike/post-beach snack. The bakery here puts out a ton of great pastries and cakes & sweets. One of my favorite places to stop by for a snack. If you're feeling extra hungry, check out the grill. Lots of great local foods to keep you stuffed all day long. No outside seating due to COVID, so grab & go is your only option.

Great Akai Bowls and more. The place is not fancy and only 2 small tables outside but is really worthy your stop. The Acai bowls are really good but you can try a lot of more like as Hummus or Avocado Toast. I won't miss if I were you.

I know this is what amount a to the local sports bar but the beer was a little flat, the fries were soggy and the burger was way over cooked with a stale jun toasted to cover the staleness. I might go for a second chance just to make sure I didn't hit a bad day for them but others have said they had similar experiences. I guess if you drink enough you don't notice the food is bad.

Came in for lunch, I ordered the fish and chips and my husband ordered a sandwich. The service was good, the AC was nice, but the food was just okay. This wasn't the best fish and chips, and the sandwich was pretty standard. They don't seem to use local ingredients or even care to try to engage local. The prices don't support that this place looks more like a dive bar than a high end restaurant. I ordered the 'famous mai thai' and got the tiki glass and even the mai thai was sort of phoned in and substandard. At this price, I would say avoid this place as there are so many better restaurants that use local ingredients nearby that we should just boycott so these guys go out of business and can get replaced with a place that can manage to jack up the quality to match the prices.

Comfort food. Giant portions. Fried rice and meat for breakfast. Great decor and service, my only issue with large portions is you sacrifice quality for quantity and I would rather have quality. Despite the huge portions they dislike ohana and charge 4 dollars to share a plate with someone else. I don't think that is very Hawaiian. Minus 2 stars for that oversight.

This is a offshoot of the Pig and Lady and definitely not to be missed. I've tried most of the menu, including the vegetarian and everything is outstanding. But let me focus on the vegetarian because the meat here is going to be amazing just because of the name.

They have a vegetarian pho broth that is very tasty and satisfying. It has hints of fives pice and garlic and definitely tasted amazing.

We also tried the mushroom pho french dip and this worked excellently as the mushrooms were coated in BBQ sauce and made for a texture like a sloppy joe. So delicious and completely vegetarian.
Best burger on the island. Get the avocado with it too.

Ken's has gone waaaaaay down hill.  I'm sure this review will get lost amongst the praise.  I've been coming here religiously for years.  Every time I come to Hilo, I head to Ken's right off. Gets the visit started right.  But this last visit was the worst and bad on so many levels I know it wasn't just an off day.  Everything was bland and prefab. The burger was akin to something you'd get at a hospital cafeteria.  The Vietnamese ice coffee was just watery ice coffee with a smear of condensed milk down the side of the glass. It was really gross and while I know it's a outlier for a diner I figured, "hey it's Hawai'i, it could be good".  Man, was I wrong.  My companion got their Thanksgiving special, the usual fair, turkey, mashed potatoes, gravy and a salad.  It looked like they had prepared plates ahead of time and microwaved them to order.  It was so pasty and bland.  The salad was just some anemic shredded iceberg from a bag with generic dressing.  We know Ken's isn't a fancy restaurant, it's a local casual diner.  But it used to be delicious and now it's over priced and bland. 😧

Full of mystery and allure.. the Spirits of the trees calling on the soft upcountry breezes of Tutu Nature, Our Mother of the Forest, naturally inviting..
Geckos and Pueo and high flying long wingspan seabirds hang on thermals in the zenith directly above.. scents of Rainbow Eucalyptus refreshes my lungs and I am aware of my breath, the air surrounding me and the silence of my mind..
Every hue of green reveals itself in monochromatic shades of olive, kelly, forest and lime greenery...
Ancient trails are disclosed and my imagination soars .. I didn’t bring my phone (camera) as I wanted a pure non-technological nature-centered experience.
While meditating in the Forest Reserve I let go and I let Lono guide me to a small waterfall and white long tailed Tropic birds sang their birdsongs and set the mood as two of them danced their way in short figure 8 sprints high in the Winter sky of upcountry Hilo.. a white faced barn owl made it’s appearance at 6: o’clock, just as the sky grew darker fast as the sunset beyond the mauna- on the Kona side sending ribbons of pink clouds streaking through Waimea’s direction and lead me out of the restricted Reserve along a thin trail back to the side of the road where I had parked hours ago..
Taking pictures would have distractingly left me tapping buttons and it would have been a disservice to my soul internally and a vexation to my inner calmness..
Thank you for reading this far and kindly LIKE this if you did!

Absolutely beautiful, awesome and cool. The Hilton Waikoloa is ocean front  and plan to spend a day seeing all it has to offer. First when you arrive you can self park for 5 dollars per hour or pay a flat rate for the whole day or valet park. From there go into the main lobby which is a combo of open air and air conditioning with a beautiful layout with great artwork and floral settings. Right through the lobby you have mini stores to buy items you might need for the night or beach, drinks, snacks and many other items. Take the walk ways and enjoy the beautiful walking trials or how about taking the tram to go to other parts of the Hilton Resort or even a boat that has inside and outside seating to get out of the sun. Your eyes will be wondering everywhere with the aquatic life throughout the resort (fish, dolphin's, sea turtles, birds, lizards and more) the tropical layout is nothing but beautiful and the ocean breeze accompanies the beautiful setting. There are lagoons, sand beach areas, open ocean. Try the zip line, paddle boarding, paddle boats, floats, swimming and many other features Hilton offers. Take a break in the restaurant or outside bar area while watching the dolphin's swim and jump out of the water. The rooms are clean and close to everything whether you want a simple view, pool view, ocean view or garden view (pretty much a view anywhere you stay). The staff is super friendly and the rates are fair for the area and Hawaii. Close to Kona and other local beaches. The Hilton is the place to stay and enjoy Hawaii. Aloha

Let's rate this review from start to finish...

The initial walk in was really good, greeted and sat almost immediately. I guess Tuesday isn't quite a busy night, as there were many open tables, booths and bar type seatings.

That's where things went a little slow on service, the girl who sat us asked what we wanted to drink. As we were giving our drink orders, she had to abruptly leave, when she came back she couldn't remember even one drink from our order, so we had to start all over. I have no idea where she had to run off to, but at least she came back..

After that ordering is always a bit humbug at this location because the iPads that they use to be able to take orders is attached to the table.

When sitting on the outside part of the table, it is very inconvenient to have to reach over to look at the menu, they don't even have the regular laminated paper menus to look at.

Aside from that, ordering stuff is usually pretty easy, select what you want and press the order button... Another thing that bothers me, is that you don't know what is in the sushi rolls, you just have to look at the picture and try to figure it out, and it's not like you can bring the iPad close to your face to examine the picture, you kind of just have to know...

Another thing I noticed about genki is that a lot of their roll sushi is the same, but they just have different toppings, which I find a little bit strange, but I never really noticed before..

Also, after we ordered a few things, which you can only order 4 things at a time, but continuously if you wish, we were brought something we didn't order... We had to double check what we did order just in case someone accidently added it on.. but we didn't, and had to call the server back. Which there is a convenient button on the iPad.

The girl promptly came back and took back the wrong order... But makes you wonder if they're paying attention...

The sushi was good, rolls, sashimi, sukiyaki udon, french fries, poke bowl, and and nigiri, so overall food was good.

But service was lacking a bit, we made a drink order through the iPad and it never came... We waited 10 mins...after we tried the service button... We tried it three times before someone came to check up on us. What was annoying about that situation, was that there were at least 5 to 6 girls standing by the drink station....

Overall food was good, but service brought the stars down...

Great beach, sand is nice, the bathroom is close by, there is good quality showers. I love it when the sand doesn't feel like pebbles and it is not super fine like powder.

The reef is a bit close to shore which might be a deal breaker, but it can be good for snorkeling. There are Tide pools, but they're on super sharp and rocky area. Wear slippers, water shoes, something to protect your feet.

Lots of parking and there is the lifeguards out to watch for people in need of help.

I used this to hike the Kuliouou Ridge Trail, which is very nice. The trail is about 5 miles round trip, has excellent views of the valley and some nice views of the ocean, and wasn't crowded. There is a resting area a little more than halfway up, and I saw someone jogging the trail.
Regarding the trailhead, it's easy to find, but it's in a residential neighborhood,  so parking is a problem. Depending on the day of the week and how many other people are already hiking, you may need to park several hundreds of yards downhill, which would add to your hike!

It's the Hymane Society, so everyone knows what they're about. Kennels are reasonably sized, and clean. There are a couple of newer buildings -- dog house blue and cat house blue . Sadly, that means they closed their dog park 🙁

I love Buca di Beppo. They offer enormous servings  of very good Italian food, and the Honolulu location is a quirky warren of rooms on the ground floor of Ward Entertainment Center. While they dont actually serve meatballs the size of your head, spaghetti with a gigantic meatball seems to be the signature dish. All the dishes come in two sizes -- large and very large. I particularly like the linguini frutti di mare, but the kids love the baked pasta and the oversized desserts. Parking in the Ward garage is inadequate, especially on a Friday or Saturday evening, but there is plenty of parking in the next building, in the Whole Foods garage.
Being close to the Ward theaters, Dave & Busters, and all the Ward shops, this is also a good location for walking around, before or after dinner.

The luxe theater was very nice. I've been there twice, and the experience was very good both times. The ticket lines can be long at Ward, but that wasn't a problem (buy your tickets ONLINE, people), the seating is extremely comfortable, and the sound and picture quality were good. If the screen were bigger, this might be the best movie theater experience in Hawaii.

Ate at Gen bbq today. It's all-you-can-eat and cook your own at the table. It was $14.99 per person for lunch drinks not included.

I'll start off by saying that, like all Korean food, it was salty but in a good flavorful way. I focused on the kalbi and Hawaiian beef steak dishes. Both had so much flavor and I could taste the fattieness of the cuts. We also tried the shrimp and brisket. These were ok. If you're looking for a place to over stuff yourself this is it. The wait help was always around to bring more dishes and replace the grilltops. The grilltops get crusted fast from the caramelized sauce. If they don't change them out fast enough they'll smoke and then flame up. I saw this happen at the neighboring table. It was good for entertainment.

One thing to keep in mind is that you will smell like Korean bbq the rest of the day. The smoke and the oil will get all over you and your clothes, thus the 4 stars rating.

We stopped by the Cheesecake Factory for a late lunch/early dinner. We know better than to go during the peak meal times since we could wait an hour or longer and we just wouldn't have the patience for it. Needless to say we sat down almost immediately. Our wait help greeted us, a young female with a fantastic smile and personality to match. I immediately thought to myself that this is someone who does very well in this line of work. But just our luck after she took our drink orders she informed us that her shift was ending and someone else would serve our table. She introduced him to us and it seemed like the energy simply left. The new waiter just didn't have the personality to match and it was a let down. He wasn't attentive and worked like he didn't care. He preferred talking with his coworkers at the bar. Sad.

Insofar as the food is concerned it was what we expected. Hamburgers and fries that were the usual juicy and flavorful dishes. Thumbs up. I'll also add that I tried something new for me a mint tea beverage. It was refreshing and tasty and I want to recommend this to everyone. You will really enjoy it!

Still have great items from my childhood days.

Well, what can I say. It is a clean and stylish up-scale high-end mall with a number of shops designed to impress you (the main entrance even sports a full-on Telsla dealership showroom no less). For those of us who grew up here when the original IMP occupied this same space -  a place we could come in from a morning on the waves and grab a quick tasty lunch from the many choices in the affordable food court (affordable for LOCALs, not just the gilded pursed tourists) - today's IMP is in name only.
- To me it would have been better if they just did as is now taking place with the old historic King's Village - they completely tore it down and will replace it with a new modern structure (a 31 story Condo thank you - much better than some small little fleeting memory of Hawaiian culture.... sarcasm people, sarcasm). That's exactly what happened here with the IMP except they retained the name for marketing reasons. Really?? If you are going to blatantly gut something at least give it the dignity of a respectful burial and bury they name with it before your charge foward in your quest to worship the almighty $$$.
- So what was the question? How do I feel about the new "IMP"? The new edifice needed a different name.

I hadn't been to this place for what seems like a few years and boy what a difference! I stopped coming here because the quality of the food had declined and they no longer warranted my business. Just by chance my usual ramen restaurant was having noodle cooker problems and my niece recommended we come back here,  as we used to do a few years back. I was hesitant, but I was game. Maybe id try some of my old favorites,  I thought. It was 6pm and they weren't busy which made me more hesitant, being seated immediately.  But when I opened up my menu, I was quite surprised that it had changed, drastically!! I don't know how long ago it did, but I wanted to try out these new (to me) menu items. My first choice was Chu-toro Sashimi... sold out from Mothersday (the day before) 😑 ok, Truffle Char Siu Roll (Truffle sushi rice, cream cheese and avocado wrapped with peppery char siu, marinated in spicy ponzu and dressed with tobiko) sounds good. What else, Char-Siu Pork & Japanese Scallions (Japanese-style barbecue pork slightly torched to perfection accompanied by japanese scallions and homemade ponzu sauce). And to round out my Char-Siu trio I had the Shokudo Char-Siu Ramen (Ramen noodles in soy tonkotsu broth, topped with garlic, bean sprouts and 5 sliced char siu pork). It was a Char-Siu lovers dream come true. Each slice of Char-Siu was as juicy and as succulent as the last, the flavor never gave up and kept delivering explosions of flavor with every mouthful. The Char-Siu pork with scallions were amazing, the Truffle Roll melted in your mouth and the Ramen was freaking amazing. The Tonkatsu broth didn't disappoint,  the only glaring mistake was in the cooking of the noodles. A little overcooked and some of the noodle were still stuck together! What was this cake noodle ramen? Still though,  10 out of 10, would eat all this stuff again, and again.

(Translated by Google) Kaiona Beach Park is located on the southeast coast of Oahu Island, and the adjacent Waimanalo Beach Park and Bellows Field Beach Park form the longest beach on Oahu Island. Kaiona means fascinating ocean in Hawaiian, and it is indeed worthy of its name. When the weather is clear, the sea is really beautiful and very charming. In particular, there are some remaining seawalls in the sea here, which block the waves. The seawater in the seawalls is very clear, and the sand at the bottom is also good. It is especially suitable for swimming and relatively safe. In the seawall is the pond where the former chiefs raised sea turtles for food. The park is equipped with parking lots, toilets, changing rooms, picnic tables, and shower facilities. There are also many trees on the beach to provide shade, but there are no lifeguards. There are usually fewer people here, and tourists rarely visit this beach, but on weekends and holidays, there are all nearby residents, and parking spaces are tight. In addition, Obama has a mansion behind the seawall under construction, and the Obamas will be residents here in the future.

©️Hawaiian tour guide photographer Youli Zhao WeChat: HiHonolulu

(Original)
Kaiona Beach Park位于欧胡岛的东南海岸，与相邻的Waimanalo Beach Park以及Bellows Field Beach Park组成了欧胡岛上最长的一个海滩。Kaiona在夏威夷语里的意思是迷人的海洋,这里也确实名副其实，天气晴朗时，海真的非常的漂亮，非常的迷人。特别的是这里的海里还有一些残存的海堤，它们阻挡了海浪，海堤里的海水非常清澈，水底的沙也不错，特别适合游泳，也相对安全。海堤里是以前的酋长养海龟来食用的池塘。公园配有停车场、厕所、更衣室、野餐桌以及淋浴设施，海滩上也有许多树可以遮阴，不过没有救生员。这里平时的人比较少，游客很少会到这个海滩，但一到周末和节假日就全是附近的居民，车位比较紧张。另外奥巴马有一栋位于海堤后面的豪宅正在建设中，奥巴马夫妇未来将是这里的居民。

©️夏威夷导游摄影师Youli赵 微信：HiHonolulu

Amazing snorkelling. Take the 22 bus from Waikiki instead of driving, because parking will always be an issue. That's only $2.75 or $5.00 return. It's going to cost $7.50 to enter the park( and you can return for a snack and then go back down), and you watch a mandatory video ( about 10 minutes) to introduce the bay. The trolley to the beach is 1.75 or 2.50 return. Or you can walk the way down.

You can rent snorkelling gear if you have not brought your own, or rent flippers if you have the rest. I recommend flippers at least, as the currents in the most interesting places will tire you out if you aren't used to it.

The fish are abundant, and not really afraid of humans. There are many areas to explore.

It's always busy, so you won't get away from people, but there's a lot of room.
At the top of the bay there is a gift shop and snack shop, but I strongly recommend taking plenty of fluids and something to eat while you are down at the beach.

I love Costco and their gas is one of the reasons. It's always significantly cheaper than the other places in the area.

If you have a membership, this is definitely the place to get gas in Hawaii.

I love Costco. We usually stop at them when we're on vacation too.

Daniel K Inouye International Airport (formerly Honolulu International Airport) is roughly 50 years behind the times, and it shows. Areas big and small were under construction, but I'm very doubtful any major changes will make this place more efficient or easier to navigate. In fact, all recent renovations seem superficial.

My flight checked in at Counter 4 after which we had to go to Gate 33, but the TSA checkpoint nearest 33 was closed. So we had to walk a hundred feet past Counter 5 to find an open one. Once past TSA we had a choice of walking all the way to our gate -- no moving sidewalks -- or take the Wiki Wiki shuttle. We waited 5 minutes in an unconditioned bus stop (on the building roof!) for a shuttle that looked far older than anything in our Handivan or City Bus system. There were only 4 seats in our (one cab) bus, the rest is standing room only, no kidding. Are those things supposed to be electric? I have never heard an electric bus or a Tesla car make that much noise! At our gate the airline offered complimentary Sara Lee pound cake and drinks (juice, soda, ice water) ... perhaps it was in apology for our flight being delayed 2:20 hours. There appears to be wifi (secured HNLWiFi and unsecured HNL Free Wifi) but I couldn't connect to either one. Nowhere inside the airport does it say that non-subscribers can connect to "Boingo Hotspot" after watching an ad. At my departure gate, power outlets are rare. Only 3 TV sets and relatively small at 20" by 10".

On my return trip, the gate we landed at had maybe 3 moving sidewalks to the main terminal and immigration processing. A dozen self-serve computer terminals are now installed, and the ticket with your photo and scanned passport (Americans only) is supposed to take the place of the customs declaration form. In the baggage claim area it is windowless just like the immigration room. Luggage carts nor restrooms are not easy to find. After you leave the customs area you are almost immediately outside-- no restaurants, waiting and greeting area, hotel courtesy desks or car rental counters. Just a pair of shabby restrooms and a couple dozen tourist pamphlets in an unmanned kiosk. Ouside you hope to flag down a taxi captain for a ride. If not you better have your bearings for an Uber or Lyft, or (like me) have a friend willing to pick you up.

I found buried on a state website when the airport was built: 1962. No wonder all the changes they try to make seem to be window dressing! When is the State of Hawaii going to rebuild this dump??

I thought this was a reataurant (like Regal Bakery before it) but actually this is a miniature food court. It's a large space with sit down tables but no booths. Ambience is noisy, you can hear the coversation at the next table. A TV behind the bar is playing '80s music videos, notably a-ha's Take On Me and Queen's Bohemian Rhapsody.

The issue for me was the almost interminable wait for the food...there was a sign stating 30-45min wait...are you kidding? Anyway, we had to take the plates home because of the wait and by the time I tried a shrimp it was already getting cold so my rating was "it's okay but nothing special". The pie is special, thus the 4 star rating.

Looks like a nice sports bar with a pretty extensive menu...most of my party (of 10) had one of the four teishoku plates.. i tried the mochiko chicken with tempura for $13.95... it was fine but where were the pickled vegetables, miso soup, et al? In absentia I'm afraid... i would go back for drinks and a burger but not for Japanese food.

We tried this restaurant after reading positive  reviews...it's quite a walk from the center of downtown Honolulu so our hopes were high for a positive experience. The pho comes in 3 sizes, medium, large and extra large. Two of us opted for the large combination while the third member of our party tried a beef / vegetable plate sans rice or noodles. My initial impressions were good; the broth is clean and has a depth of flavor without being overseasoned. The noodles weren't overdone...my only negative were the protein... not much flavor...just okay. Was it good? Yes, it was. Would I go back when there are other pho restaurants closer to our office? Maybe not...perhaps another visit is in order. It was full when we got there at 12:40...

A friend and I met at Basalt as dine-in ends at midnight for at least two weeks (but many of us think it will be longer). We started with Hendrick's and tonic, then tried to order the Spicy Kampachi Poke (a winner!) but they just ran out...so we ordered Ahi Poke and Rotisserie Duck Empanadas to start. The Ahi Poke was fine (but not special) and the empanadas were okay... I think the aged goat cheese overwhelmed the other ingredients (brandied cherry and pickled jalapeños). It was served atop what depicted as avocado lime crema but I thought I tasted mustard.  We followed these up with the Pork Belly Buns...the pork was great and it came with pickled vegetables like a banh mi...it was served in charcoal bao buns; they tasted okay but the color was off-putting to me. All in all, a reasonable deal for good, but not special food. I've had better on prior visits...perhaps the fact that they were shutting down (again) due to the pandemic affected the food. The service was fine. Let's hope they're still around to re-open.

What can I say the experience was amazing. My husband surprised me for my birthday and took me to the top of Waikiki. This was my first time going to this restaurant. simple me didn't know at the restaurant spins. Upon being seated I noticed that the restaurant was spinning. Our waitress was extremely tentative and very polite. She recommended items from the menu as well as introduced the specials of the day. Upon taking our drink and appetizer order you placed our order for entrees. I ordered the pork chop with the house mashed potatoes and my husband ordered some kind of seafood dish. everything was cooked to perfection extremely tasty compliments to the chef. we decided to get dessert and order the deconstructed apple pie with the vanilla gelato. That dessert was amazing. Not too sweet and not too heavy. Everything about our experience was simply amazing. I will definitely come back here to celebrate it an occasion again.

Spectrum TV and cable internet service is not what I thought it would be. I have had Hawaiian telcom for 4 years. My 4 year promotion with HTC was coming to an end and i wanted to see what they hype was with Spectrum. I purchase the same TV package or equivalent to what I had with Hawaiian telcom.  I had the one gig Internet with  HTC and I downgraded to the 400 with spectrum. What I found that didn't work for me and my family is spectrums DVR service. You literally have to record your TV show or movie on every Box if you want to watch it throughout your house. It isn't like Hawaiian telcom home DVR service where you can access your DVR in any room.  Another downfall was their Internet speed. My home alarm worked poorly on spectrums Internet. Extremely poorly. To the point where videos and the doorbell camera did not work it buffered constantly. It only took me several days to realize I made a huge mistake. I returned all of spectrums equipment And I cancel their service and went back to Hawaiian telcom. I should have never left in the 1st place. Lesson learned. If it ain't broke don't fix it. I will never be a spectrum customer again.

Always a great experience at Haleiwa Joe's in Kaneohe! We went shortly after the COVID-19 restrictions were lifted to celebrate a friend's retirement. Dinner was fantastic, and the night was a great time.
The restaurant was able to seat us in 30 minutes... Fastest ever for Haleiwa Joe's, which doesn't take reservations. Tables are set up with appropriate distancing, servers are wearing masks, and the staff was doing a great job sanitizing the tables and chairs after guests left.
The menu was great, as usual. It had changed since I had been there a year ago, but still had plenty of amazing offerings. Kudos to the Exec Chef.
Price was appropriate for 4-star dining, and we got the great experience we expected. Check for two was $120, dinner and drinks.
I highly recommend Haleiwa Joe's. Get there before 6pm, if you want a chance at their prime rib (specialty if the house)!

Located right next to McKinley Car Wash on Kapiolani (ooooh, dance fight!). Superior in quality, cleanliness, speed, and service. And their monthly pass is such a value!

PROCESS:
1. Drive up to one of 2 gate arms (slowly if you got their monthly pass RFID tag, so the sensors can pick up the tag's signal).
2. If you are a 1x wash, select wash & any add-ons, and pay automated machine (if you got their monthly pass, you don't have to do anything unless you want an add-on service - - in fact, you don't even have to lower your window!)
3. Drive forward & to the right.
4. An employee guides your car to the pre-scrubbing area for extra cleaning to your car's nose & tires.
5. Put your car in neutral, turn off any automatic windshield wipers, make sure your windows are rolled up, and put your vents to incoming air (vs AC/recycled) if you got the Super Fresh wash, because their scent bomb addition makes your car smell awesome for days!
6. Enjoy the disco lights! Entertain the kids!
7. At the end of the wash, watch the little stoplight click from "wait" to "go" and follow the employee to the hand-drying area.
8. Sit in your car for the hand-dry, or get out and stretch your legs.
9. Done! Be dazzled. ;)

Quick wash time w/thorough machines. Strong blow dryer  and excellent tire scrubbers. The hand-dry afterwards used clean microfiber towels that looked like ones you'd have at your house, not grimy ones like some other places. Got the Super Fresh wash and the wax coating had water beading off my windshield. Love!

Stay in your car the entire way through, great if you have little kids w/carseats--no hasty unbuckling or herding away from driveway death--& they'll love the disco light show. The wash corridor also seemed longer than other automated places. Foam was thick and even, and the water was clean--no gray spots or break in the foam. Quality!

Everyone was in a neat & clean uniform. Staffed by local young men & women working super crazy hard (those not helping customers were busy sweeping the driveway, polishing equipment, etc.) who seem to love cars. Exactly the kind of place I want to take my car! Oddly, there is no tip jar, like at other places. My guess is they're trying to avoid  any uncomfortable pressure to tip. Still, if there's anybody I'd like to tip, it's these folks--the hand-dry afterwards was detailed and so good. Service!

Done in minutes w/a result would have taken me at least an hour at home. But no buying all these carwash products that I have to store, cleaning a bunch of chamois afterwards, or getting on a step stool to wax the roof. Sanity-saver!

Manage your monthly pass or buy/gift car washes through their app. No more getting out of your car at a sketchy gas station to get cash for some busted kiosk (eek!), no more problems keeping track of expenses for washes, no more stress about telling a salty clerk your license plate number every time you go (ahem ahem...). Convenient!

Accepts credit cards.

Open late! Til 10 pm.

Definitely get their monthly pass, tho even their one-time purchases are very reasonable for what you get. The cheapest car washes on island are terrible; we don't have the time or space to regularly wash & wax our cars at home or the room to store all the necessary products for this kind of clean; & won't regularly pay $100+ a wash for those detailed washes. This is the perfect sweet spot--value, quality, efficient, convenient, great customer service. YAAAAASSSSS!

They may have more of a homeless problem here though as we were approached by one asking for bus fare and two others were standing outside.  Not my favorite Wendys, but it is in a very convenient location. Drive through line would have alleviated all the negatives, but we prefer to dine in.

Service was good, but food was subpar.

The Fish in my fish Tacos was so sparse that all together it would be less than a McDonald's Filet of fish, but the worse thing about it was it was so tough, dry and overcooked. I have never had such rubbery tough fish in my life. Will never order that again.

The Strawberry Shortcake looked spectacular and the fresh strawberries and sauce were delicious, the ice cream and whipped cream with lemon zest was excellent... However all was spoiled by the super dry, hard and old biscuit. Such a waste. My son and I had to eat around it. The flavor was good on the biscuit, but it was definitely stale.

It was a very expensive subpar meal for our family... Will think hard before returning...

I go here all the time as a local. I do highly recommend putting on sunscreen and re applying. Also, if can, park on the beach side and hold on to your belongings. There's strong currents and rip currents too. So only experienced swimmers should be swimming in these conditions. Please respect the ocean's power.

I have stayed here twice for Kawaii Kon, they offer a good deal rate for attending the convention and it's within walking distance of the center. The rooms are small but nice, the showers have good pressure. The convenience store is well stocked. The hotel is easy to access by road but parking is tight. I paid for parking but I spent 20 minutes finding an open spot in a one level underground garage. Might be easier to park in the Mall next door.

I have never been to a Cheesecake factory and now that I have, I dont feel compelled to go again. That does not mean I have a negative opinion, just that once was enough. There is nothing wrong with this establishment, the service was good (but a little slow) we went with a group of 7 and took nearly 2 hours to get through the whole lunch and ended up taking the cheesecake to go after our meals. The servings were very generous and the restaurant is easy to find and park at. It's right next to the mall so you can follow up with some shopping or walking. All that said I wouldn't make a habit of driving across island for this place.

Lobster wasn't cooked entirely and tasted like lychee soaked in water. The steak was also tasteless. Drown it in A1 sauce then it tasted fine. Service was good for the first twenty minutes then it sort of died down after a few larger partied showed up. Our server didnt show up to check on us until we were ready to get our check.

I ordered the snow crabs combo and substituted mussels with extra pound of shrimp. The bag came within 10 minutes of wait. Hardly seasoned even though we asked for spicy. There's no sausage in the bag though every combo bag should come with sausages. There's about 7 shrimps in it and that was supposedly be 2 pounds of shrimp? On top of that, crab legs were stone cold. Sent the bag back and it came back reheated with some sausages but still 7 shrimp. Pretty ridiculous actually. The waitresses were just on their phones and hanging out behind the registers. I understand there's not much going on in the restaurant so maybe not much else to do. But if you can't even get one order right out of an almost empty restaurant, you got a problem.

Instead of circling the lot with twenty other cars who's looking for parking you might as well just park at Walmart and walk. Whoever put the "biggest foodland on the island" never thought about the parking situation???

Offered to pay with a visa debit card. $50 total on card, ask to apply all to bill and would leave cash for tip. Server(Barbara) came back and stated card automatically takes 20% tip if applied and only could use $40. We stated ok we will use the remainder ($10) later in retail and left cash tip. Reviewed the transactions and did not see either listed on the charge or handwritten on the receipt anywhere about the $8.00 automatically tip! Took the card and when to retail store thinking the card had $10 remaining. Presented the card and surprised to find only had $2.00 remaining. I would like to think the server could have either figured out how to only apply the $40. Use the debit verse the cc settlement and keyed in the $50 and could have listed the amount. Ended up with a 35% tip. O’well learned how to use visas debits.

Beautiful, buy potentially dangerous. The path starts at the top of Punahele Road (there is a small parking lot and signs pointing to the trailhead), and winds its way down to the shoreline. The relatively short trail is full of roots and mud on many days, and is not best for sandals. There are a few signs warning people of the dangers of Queen's Bath and they should not be ignored. Stay a safe distance from the edges, never turn your back to the water and don't even bother in high surf. However, the view is spectacular when the weather and sea permit.

Beautiful spot on the north coast of the Big Island. However, you cannot get down to the beach without a 4x4 or a significant hike. Most people just look from afar, and it you're familiar with the north coasts of other Hawaiian islands, you will know that it can get quite rainy at times.

Volcanoes National Park rangers were there giving a talk about the current eruptions. I found it very informative and interesting. The displays were very well done, educational and the kids enjoyed.

Awesome , very informative Visitor Center inside Hawaii Volcano National Park, huge place, with hiking, camping, Hotel, cabins, Restaurants, and guided tours.  The entrance fee is similar to other National Parks, but periodically they will have a free day (such as September 26, 2019), and if you volunteer to help out on the free day, they will give you a free pass to come back on another day.  Several souvenir shops, but with pricy stuff.
Very good fish dishes, but not a good place for steaks.  Family-owned & operated for over 100 years (at least 3 generations).  7-9 am breakfast, 11-2 lunch, 5-7 dinner.

Friendly, unique atmosphere, quality rich broth, very good ramen. Nice condiments, add-in options. Parking sometimes challenging due to other busy businesses in the same mini mall/building, but usually fine. I go off hours, and it's perfect.

Central, nice selection of shops and restaurants, but the parking is changed quite a bit with the significant development over the last several years and is more unique to each section of the center than it used to be. More than any other shopping center in Hawaii, it has something for everyone including a nearly 50,000 square foot Foodland supermarket that will soon open beneath Nordstrom. Ala Moana should be an essential first terminus for the new rail system until the political will and funding is found to extend it to the middle of Waikiki.

Spacious, organized, fairly good selection of local produce and organic produce. Much better than the old Safeway of yore. If you shop carefully you can save quite a bit participating in their members Club. Plentiful parking with monkey pod trees that are finally filling out. The single constant tenant in this mini mall. Their floral department occasionally has great buys.

Good location, nice screen size, comfortable seats, good sounds system, and participates with MoviePass. Lots of parking although it's a bit of a hassle when busy. Wide ranging junk food offerings at high prices. Should offer furikaked popcorn.

Serviceable, good Cantonese food, dim sum. Fairly large space with banquet tables, take out area near the cashier in front, in the heart of old Chinatown next to Oahu market.Cake noodle was salty, chicken overcooked. Lo bok gau was pan-fried well, but not tender. Wu gok/taro puffs were huge, fried alright and the filling was okay. Coconut jin doi were tasty but the mochi was not tender and they were lukewarm, but that was likely because it was mid afternoon. I would like to try them again at peak time between 11:00 and 1:00.

The venue, with high ceiling and open walls to the south and west, with territorial views of the coastline, is incomparable. There are nice fire pits to sit around while imbibing and noshing. Their offerings are variable, some excellent, others just good. Prices are moderately high. Good beverage offerings. Parking can be challenging during busy times, but there is an overflow lot further makai. Happy hour is from 3:00 to 5:00 p.m. with some breaks on the pricing.

The Shack is a go-to always. I first went back in 2003 and not a single thing has changed with the crowd, staff, or food. Wait staff is always perfectly friendly and respectful, food and menu is typical and good bar grub, while crowd is dependent on the time of day (between work, military, and old timer crews).

This is a really fun place to go with friends. The food is phenomenal, and not too pricey but also not exactly cheap. The employees are so kind and provide excellent service.  This place is very accommodating.

Always a great spot for breakfast with hot coffee, regular or decaf. Time for food prep is decent and good seating while waiting for food to cook. Would visit again.

Multiple-park sections for windsurfers as well as traditional surfers. Picnic tables, showers and public bathroom with handicap parking and facilities. Shady coconut groves and Sandy beaches a mile long. Near airport in Kahului- adjacent.

Awesome awesome awesome ! The curry rice with the braised pork belly was incredible ! Ooh full on Japanese style beef curry , and steamed rice .
The staff needs a little bit of polishing up to do . When I placed my order I had politely requested no macaroni salad and my lunch companions had requested additional sides to their order of ramen . When the meals were presented there was a few snags . My order came with the macaroni salad , they took it back and scraped the Mac salad off and put the rice on . On the other orders it took a while to get the requested sides to be brought to the table . There were a few condiments available the obligatory soy sauce , white pepper , pickled ginger , toasted sesame seeds , and some togarashi Japanese spice mix . It would have been nice to have some salt and hot sauce .
The service could have been a little better .
All in all I'd rate the food at 97/100 and the service at around 75/100 . We are willing to give it another try in the future . Hopefully it will go smoother next time .

Nice big open area. For the Pokémon go player it has several pokestops and 3 gyms in walking distance and several more in the area.

OMG! The malasadas were so good! Vanilla filled with chocolate frosting. We bought the raisin bread to bring home, smells so good! Very relaxing place to stop and sit for a while.
Very nice staff. They are waiting for a new shipment the next fre days so it was rather bare.

I love this store. It has a fabulous variety of products. It has a fabulous bakery and deli. You could spend hours in there, and we did. The prices are a little high. But, we will definitely shop there in the future.

this urban reimaginaton is an intriguing view of what Kaka'ako will become. what was once a seedy block of dilapidated buildings had been redeveloped to upscale standards. the restaurants are hip and stylish, the stores quirky and young. SALT is a fun and vibrant place to visit.

on the practical side, parking on site is reasonable and many, if not all, tenants validate parking. avoid straying too far away from Ala Moana Blvd for street parking - the unredeveloped areas can be dark and not very secure feeling
the Honolulu Zoo is an amazing oasis of nature in Waikiki. The new ecotherm is much friendlier than the old building it replaced.

Just ate lunch here today. We were very impressed with the cleanliness of the place and the decor. Very nicely decorated with a faith based theme. I had the Ko Olau Chilli relleno and it was amazing. Also, there is a flea market on premises. It was not open when we were there and I suspect it is shut temporarily due to COVID but I cannot confirm that.

Yes. It is steep, and no.  It is not a quick easy hike.  I am 5 months pregnant and out of shape.  I hiked half of this with a baby on my back before giving in and allowing a friend to carry him the rest of the way. I was out of breath and light headed.  I often leaned against trees to catch my breath and layed down for 20 minutes to let the light headedness pass... but important to note, while it might be slippery at times, there are many exposed tree roots and rocks to get your footing and almost the entire hike is shaded by trees. I agree, this hike would be absolutely ridiculous in the mud or rain. My kids 4, 5 and 8 did great... they loved it! Just when you think you can't make it any further, you're probably just a few minutes from a picnic table resting point and only about 10 or 15 minutes from the first pillbox, watch point.

This beach is great for families with smaller children as there really aren’t too many waves.  The beach sand here is nice and smooth.  The only thing I didn't like about this beach was the ocean bottom.  There were a lot of rocks under the water, but it's an easy enough fix to bring some water shoes.  The kiddo liked it because he could watch all the planes land and take off.

I would definitely come back here if we were just looking for an easy going beach day.

I usually get the same thing at any Panda's I go to.  I always get noodles, orange chicken, honey walnut shrimp, and cream cheese ragoons.  The great thing about Panda's is that no matter where I am, the food always tastes the same.

I can never find orange chicken that tastes quite like theirs.  Its this perfect combination of sweet, salty, and tanginess.    The honey walnut shrimp is awesome because its kind of like shrimp canton but with a more milder flavored sauce and the walnuts give it the right amount of sweet and crunch.  As for the cream cheese rangoons...I dont know what it is about it, but I just love them.  The creaminess with the crunch of the fried won ton gets me every time.  Honestly, its so lame, but its so yummy!

Nothing here ever blows me away and nothing is good enough that it makes it to the top of my list of things to eat when I need a quick meal.  BUT... it does make for some great comfort food.

Man McDonalds brainwashes us from when we are little.  After church my 3 year old saw the arches and was like "fries?" in his super cute little voice... how could I say no?!?!

The few times that we did stop, our food order was correct so I guess we got luck  according to all these other reviews.  LOL.

I've visited here numerous times in the last two decades and every time I make the drive through Waimea Canyon the scenery and cloud formations over the canyons are uniquely different every time.  Green, lush, red rock/dirt and the vastness are breathtakingly overwhelming.  This place is truly the "Grand Canyon of the Pacific." If you like to take photos, you will be in heaven. Be sure to a lot yourself at least a half day when you finally arrive to the park's entrance to get to Lookout Point to fully enjoy this place.

Park is well maintained. Restrooms along the way are clean and well-stocked. There are local vendors that sell water, sodas and snacks at the Point, but it's wise to bring your own food and water. Be sure to wear sunscreen.

私達は予約通りすぐに席に通して頂きましたが先に待っていた日本人2組を飛び越して、後から来た日系人の方が私達と同じテーブルに来られました。私達が英語が話せないと思ったのか隣なので色々聞こえましたがここの店の知り合いでポキをサービスされたりしていました。それくらいはいいですが、順番を飛び越すのは失礼極まりないと思います。

Not too bad for a field trip with kids. Some things remind me of home growing up. It was fun having lots of kids getting excited about everything...
The place is clean and a lot of little houses were clean and nicely maintained...Big patches of taro and not too many sugar cane...we found a star fruit and there's a lot of fruits growing.. The kids really enjoyed the tiger dance.

Going there early weekdays morning is the best way to shop..check out lanes doesn't take longer than 2min. Probably more. But it's worth it. No pumping and swerving of carts. Best thing is it's not that expensive for bulk items
Restocking was pretty slow but has no problem finding what I needed
Nice calm and clean water family oriented
Alright mall, variety of different foods available. Several large stores either on-site or very nearby.

It's pretty pricey here for certain items, but other items cheaper than whole foods. They offer a food bar, but be aware not all items are the same price so dish accordingly as they weigh it at checkout. They have a decent for the area bulk bin selection and spice rack that you can buy in whatever weighted quantity you seek.
Must wear shirts and sandals according to their sign out front... So I'm going to try no pants and see what happens.

My visits have been met with mixed success. The beer selection could be improved I have been to less specialized stores with a slightly wider selection of non-generic stuff. Plenty of main-stream choices, a few growing in popularity, but mainly just the known stuff. Not good for picking up a new/seasonal brew from your favorite micro-brewery.
The cider selection seems to be either constantly sold out of the good stuff or is never restocked.
Wine selection is decent. I usually buy $12-$25 bottles from Costco though.
Last visit the whole store smelled of slightly too old fish... So very unappealing and I did not opt to browse, just grabbed something small and checked out.

Incredibly crowded normally. But has a wide selection of food vendors. The selection of produce is also pretty large in comparison with most of the other larger farmer's market on the island.  Get there early if you want easy parking. Opens at 0730. Get there early and you cannot enter until the air horn goes off.
good food, music and friendly service.  seating is limited and sometimes a bit 'sticky'  - but food is fresh and tasty.

No question- this place raises the bar for all of Hawaii Kai -- a savvy and delicious menu, a full bar, a sports fan dream of a sports bar with monitors galore; sitting on the canal with a modern look, friendly staff and plenty of parking.  A small, but perfect menu for bar patrons, with enough to satisfy dinner patrons as well (light dinner - almost tappas style and also a great burger).  We had the wings and fish tacos along with a cabernet wine and the Hop Lei IPA --  all were excellent.  Generous portions and expertly prepared and presented.  large, med and small tables for any size party.  They also offer lunch and breakfast.  A soft open for the moment, so check Instagram and FB for kitchen hours during the week.

I really want to like them, but it's always hit and miss.  Best bets for us are the Sicilian Chicken (always good) and the Cesar salad.  The bean soup is okay most of the time, but many times, it's too thick, and once I found a long hair in mine -- ugh.  The pasta is pretty much run of the mill -- the dishes offered, but they taste pretty good.  Service is usually good, but can get really sketchy on busy nights -- plenty of free parking at this location.
Great chicken! Great quantity at great prices. Friendly staff
The best kept Ross that I have been to  Hawaii. Very clean, shelves are stocked and personnel are helpful and friendly!!
One of the most amazing hikes that lead to a very large beautiful waterfall,hikes takes you over a reservoir damn or dyke .
great place to stop and get great Hawaiian food for me ,??? Dessert first !!!
beer ice meat repeat .lol, a grocery store.where you stop before events at Aloha Stadium. dont forget the CHARCOAL.
kinda just a spot along my main moto biking route !!!

Great donuts, decent service, and good locations... But BUSY BUSY BUSY! if you're looking  to go here make sure you have some time to spare because it can take close to 20 mins just to order. 4 stars because of slow service.
Typical exchange with a friendly staff.
Small local store with a few unique items.  Local poi, small but good quality selection of sea food.  The store even smells "clean" even around the fish.  Staff is very friendly and there is parking.
Good venue, expensive for what you get but conveniently located.  Food is buffet and quality is better than expected.  The show is good, lot of down time before.  Great location.
Good spot, cheap drinks, good escape fron the nuts crowd of first fridays
Best place on the island to buy booze, Also don't forget about the poke! The staff here are awesome as well, very knowledgeable.
Grand, quite fitting name for this hotel. Humuhumu.. is one of the best restaurants I've ever been to. Great views of Hawaii and amazing place for kids to play and have fun in the water park.

Best comic shop on the island. Great group of people with good vibes at this shop.

The first time I came to play MTG at this shop I was welcomed with open arms and have been coming back ever since. If you play tabletops, cards, board games, d&d, and many more; this is the place you want to check out.

This is one of my go to spots for Japanese food in town as they have excellent sushi, tofu, ramen, and alcohol selections.  The last two times I've been here there has been a lot of Japanese tourists/residents eating here as well.  And as you know, that is always a great sign of an ethnic centered restaurant.  Chiba-Ken also has Japanese staff and sushi chefs, which is also one of my criteria for an awesome sushi restaurant (usually the older the sushi chef is tells you how good it will be!)  PRO TIP: They have a great late night menu starting at 9:30pm, sushi, tofu, apps., beer, and sake.

If you are looking for a non pretentious bar with great food and drinks. Look no further!

Harbor Pub is a dive bar with the great feeling of being transported to a different time. Whenever someone I know says let's go to Harbor Pub I will always be down to go.

Looking for food, I recommend the pizza 🍕. But they have an extensive menu and also do breakfast.

Oh. Don't forget they also have a 3-7pm happy hour!
Great environment to have a delicious sushi

Fantastic. Came here twice. Simple Hawaiian fare at reasonable prices. Plenty of parking and places to sit. Good customer service and so so many options to choose from! Really great value for money. I had the beef stew, poi, grilled Ahi (fantastic!!). But apparently they're famous for their loco moco
Good food and friendly people. This was also the only restrount in the area that we could find that was open late.
It's a clean movie theater with big seats and isn't to expensive.
The pizza is pretty good. They have a lot of toppings if you wish to create your own pizza. You can get in and out of here pretty fast. Although the craft beer is pretty pricey 7-9 dollars a glass.
One of the few hotels that allows full sized dogs. Great staff. Location is not very good, but the staff and room made up for it.
Great selection, good prices. Choose your shopping time carefully or you will end up waiting in line for close to an hour to check out.
Too crowded, too noisy, and waiters are slow.
Extremely great place to visit but don't forget to get bottle water with you.Another things was if you afraid of the height don't go up there. Also hiking trail road is kind of old so Be Careful.
This is place getting better but they need to fast and better service. Most of the time having long line.

Went to the fall harvest festival. Cute place for families and children. The pumpkins were nice, the sunflower field was beautiful, and the parking was decent. My only criticism- there was no pumpkin bread :(

If you're ONLY coming here to check out boiling pots and look over the railing, this place isn't worth your time. If you've got kids and want to have a picnic or something, there's a grassy area out front and bathrooms.
Great service and they usually have what you need. Always very helpful

Around 3:30 -  4:30, majority of the checkout checkouts are closed!!! Why?  As a result, the 2 or 3 checkouts that are open, the lines are soooooo long. Me and the other customers are always looking around wondering why the other checkout lines are closed.  Even Foodland checkout lines, majority of them our always open during the day.

I couldn't find one employee near the aisle i needed help with to locate a particular screw. I went up and down each row looking for someone.  But nope!  NOBODY available!  Tried going to customer service (2 clerks).  But they were already bombarded with other customers.  I had to go to the people in the pick up area to ask for help.  The man who helped me tried his best helping me locate the screw i needed. Good thing it was the right one. This one employee gets a 5 out of 5 stars from me.  But overall experience...2 out of 5 stars.

The garlic bread was decent.  Calamari was decent (wish they was a sauce that came with it), my custard dessert was delicious. But that beef plate thingy...it came with only 2 pieces of beef that tasted bland.  Had to add salt.  On the menu it was labeled some sort of "stew", but not even!  At least give me rice with it.
The service was awesome, food was decent.

Small shopping center not a lot of shops ample parking
Always worth the trip.  Been coming since they opened, and amazed at the growth. Not to be missed if you're visiting our islands!
Good prices, friendly service. I want a chinchilla...

it's a fun place to cast a line out , fish a little, walk around, have a picnic or something like that.  The water is usually pretty gross so getting in it for any reason or keeping any of the fish you might catch is probably a very bad idea. But all and all a nice place to get out and about.
great place to learn how to surf.  #nofishing
Cool fishing spot BUT make sure you have ur permit.

We stayed here in June 2017.  We were BLOWN AWAY with what we received!  We were given a full kitchen, 2 large bedrooms, a dining area, a large living room with room to sleep a few adults AND a balcony that was from one end of the living room to the other end of the bedroom.  We are going to Kona again in June 2018 and will be with a decent size group of about 15 families.  Hopefully they have group rates because we are there!!!!
Great food and this family has been in HK for a long time.  Love the food and you can actually order anything for take out!

This place sux, food sux, service sux so stay away!!!!  Nah nah, only joke.  Trying to keep people away from here cause it's getting super busy nowadays and I had to wait in line.  Only took 5 minutes but not used to the lines.

I was taught long ago that you are not suppose to put any sauce on good beef as it will mask the taste of the beef.  The taste of good beef will come out in the burger.  Just my opinion.

A great mall anchored by Macy's, CVS, Consolidated Theaters and California Pizza Kitchen. There's a Starbucks, Jamba Juice, and many other trendy shops providing a wide variety of merchandice. As with the Ala Moana Shopping Center it is visited by many tourists staying at near by Waikiki hotels and the Kahala Resort!
Really nice place for hike and views, busy in weekend and crowds
Always amazing and great place, they have security in parking lot and pretty organized place to visit. Have to pay fee to get inside pretty worth it.

I had the steak and shrimp tacos. The sauté shrimp impressed me the most, because it was flavorful. The steak was ok. I wish the steak was chopped in little pieces not as big as a thumb. Price is right...portions are big. Line was long...but we made it in.  The seating was for 2's or it's...any larger than that you would have to go to the bar table area. Food was above average...and it was tasty.
Free parking, clean water, friendly locals, fresh water hose to wash off, wheel chair accessible (best of any beach on Oahu).  It's a small quaint place. Lot of local families visit this place.

Mixed feelings about our meal. The fish and chips were really good so thumbs up for them. The oysters weren't great - we had them raw and they were full of grit and a couple looked pre-chewed... The worst offender was the Loco Moco - the burger patty was really overcooked even though we'd ordered it medium. The waitress was really good about it and didn't charge us for it so they did make good.
Brandon and Fernando were great, the guy that told us we could leave was rude!

My relatives visiting from Okinawa had personal items stolen from rooms on two separate occasions while staying at this hotel. I filed a police report for them during their last stay there. Be careful at this location.
Cauliflower crust was amazing! 🍕 Friendly staff at this location 😊

Don’t get me started on this beach... one of my favs. I went. Early November. White sandy bottoms. Waves were almost non existent which is weird for winter months but it was beautiful and the water was so clean and clean even when you went out you could still see the bottom. Went snorkeling more towards the middle and right side of the beach ( away from the rock) it was clear and sand didn’t get kicked up. Saw a bunch of school fish and all the way towards the right there’s rocks and a bunch of different type of fish. Even a small a baby barracuda. Super awesome. Just be careful when the swell does eventually get up! Separate facilities and showeres. Life guards on duty too :)Beach is big also even at its busiest time it was fun and people are always nice. Mixture of tourist and locals!

Very fast and great food, they don’t hold on the butter or all the good stuff. Must stop here for breakfast one day. Good prices too. Love this little gem!! You order at the front s d seat yourself. The turn over rate is fairly quick so always somewhere to sit.

Don’t have kids personally, but when I babysit I bring them here and they LOVE it. Wasn’t too busy when we have came lately. The kids love it, the food is what you expect. They have hand sanitizer wipes and liquid. Which is very much appreciated. Was a clean store and everyone was so friendly.

I love me some Dave and Busters! Came on a work event, they were great! Games are fun, can’t beat the bar right in the middle of it all! Came again for date night and sat down and eat, we are vegetarian and the Impossible burger was AMAZING. Truly was a great meal! Can’t beat their deals either with buying food and game cards together!
FOOD IS GREAT. Server could have been a bit better. But other than that. You should come stop by while you’re shopping. If you like pizza, which I know you do. Come stop by. Worth it.

Talk about YUM. This place is so good and everything made to order. The crepes and wonderful and gelato is too. They always have an assortment of great flavors. It is warm when you walk in, but don’t let that stop you! Give this place a try!

Clean, beautiful beach park. Benches and picnic tables for chilling, clean beaches for swimming and suntanning, and grassy areas for get-togethers... love this place.

One of the best Mai tais on the island. They have a nice foam float that’s fantastic. Happy hour is a good deal with both food and drinks reasonably priced.
Great place with lots of good food. Sugoi. Young's market for Hawaiian food. Kam bakery for poi donuts. Ube tarts. And bobo tea at Shaka Shaka!

Also places to shop like Jeans Warehouse and local shops. Gas station also in the same location. Bus stop in front and also, it's a tourist stop.
Alot of things to buy. Nice looking clothes for men, women, kids, and all ages and all sizes too. Lots of household things.

Good inexpensive food! I enjoyed the teri burger and salad. I also ordered the paniolo burger. I was hoping for a big pineapple slice in the burger but was a bit disappointed, of course the burger was awesome though! I was drawn to this burger because of the pineapple. Lol. The fries, which is pretty awesome and guess what? it's not French fries! Potato wedges with not too salty and just right crunch and seasonings! Get those fries yo!

Service was fast and friendly helpful staff too. I would recommend this place to anyone looking for good burger on Kahuku side. It's hidden behind the gas station. There is enough space to sit down and enjoy your food. Either alone or with a group. They have vegetarian options. Bathroom available down the stairs on the right. Debit and credit are accepted, you can either use their system to tip or to using cash. All you can drink available, so get that $2.25 drink or byod. New burger available;  Rami burger. Didn't try it but from the lips of the staff, it's pretty darn good! So order it and let us know how it goes.

I didn't wait too long for my food to be delivered, maybe about 6 mins, we had several people in front of us but we didn't wait too long. You can enjoy your food while sitting outside or inside. Thanks for the good meal! Support local yo!
plenty of tidepools. saw a couple turtle. be careful though and mind the tide. no life guard on duty.
A large store filled with all things that are delicious! So many choices, filled with a variety of meat, vegetables, and fruits! Can't forget the awesome selection of drinks too!

Drill weekend lunch. Customer service was good. Chimichanga's were maybe a little too good. Hard to stay awake in afternoon meetings. Not sure if it was just my chair, but it could have used some updating.

Location would not have bothered me when I was younger. Now as a middle aged, suburban family man driving around in a minivan, it was a little more sketchy for me. LOL
Parking is limited so we parked down the road, which magnifies the sketchiness.

Very limited parking, and the uber rich Princeville community prohibits street parking. Try to visit during off-peak hours or you'll be sorely disappointed driving all this way. There's usually a queue of running cars waiting for a parking space to free up, which is definitely more damaging to the environment than allowing cars to park on the side of the road. This island site is the definition of privileged access.

(Translated by Google) Sandy beach located on the east side of Oahu, Hawaii. It is a spot where you can surf and body board. It takes about 30 minutes by car from Waikiki. This beach is popular with locals, and is popular with locals on weekends. There is a lot of parking, but it's a busy weekend. I recommend going from morning if you go.
The tide is strong here, so please refrain from swimming when the waves are large or the wind is strong.
As food trucks gather at the weekend, it is not troubled with rice and drinks.
By the way, the stars are very beautiful when going at night. However, please be careful because security is not very good.

This Macy's is great for shopping for regular items, not so much for specialized items such as suits or formal wear.  Their prices are excellent and you can find some pretty cheap deals in their "Last Act" section. I found some nice Calvin Klein dress shirts normally priced at $49, but was discounted to $7.99 - awesome deals!  Not a full 5 stars due to the fact that they have a limited selection of specialty items such as suits and formal wear.  The closest option would be Ala Moana, but I don't drive to that side of the island too often.

I live on the North Westside of the island and find myself very rarely at Kahala Mall.  I enjoy the mall itself and the nice variety of shops and stores located in and around the mall.  As with ALL malls, parking is particularly tight and can take some time to find decent parking. Even the parking at the outskirts of the mall are filled up.

Arrive early to find parking and please please don't wait in the middle of the lane while you scope out parking, be considerate to allow other drivers to drive around your waiting car.

Wow! This place is amazing! I haven't been here for years and the food hasn't changed much.  It's definitely the best place to get the best bang for your buck! The food here is very ono-licious!

If you're vegan, you'll find it very difficult to eat here - but honestly ANY plate lunch place is catered to carnivores.  Any-who this place is great for eating and falling into a food coma right after.  Great place for grinds!

Just finished the Great Aloha Run, and we started at the Stadium to park our car and ran back to drive home.  The parking situation at the Stadium can pretty confusing sometimes - it's a good thing that the stadium staff was there or I'm sure you would have had some lost wandering vehicles roaming around.  The lines to the parking in some areas are quite faded and can make it difficult to know where the parking starts or ends.  Overall experience was good, and the layout of parking and bus pick up worked great!

Recently went here to shoot a wedding, and I must say the staff here are pretty "mean" like in a bad way.  I wasn't necessarily shooting the wedding, rather I was grabbing cards for a same-day-edit later in the evening.  I placed myself outside so that I wouldn't take up some seating.  I sat outside by the elevators - - where I was then asked to sit behind a current instead (because I guess, I was in the way?) - - after sitting behind a current for about 4 mins, a staff member told me to smile as their "Japanese guests" walked by. I understand that this is a business, but c'mon I'm a guest too!

I didn't feel welcomed despite all the "fake" smiles I received. Last time I was here - - was when it was strictly a restaurant . Now I have no urge to come back or even recommend this spot as a wedding venue.  The staff here are just straight rude.

I've lived here for 30+ years and have only recently tried out this place and I have to say it was superb in taste and price! It only took a native Oregonean to introduce me to this place. I normally only like to eat Hanalei Poi, but wherever I go it's always sold out. The poi here was very good - not too lumpy or too sour. It had the right amount of flavor and wasn't overly sweet. The kalua pig was also very moist and wasn't overly salty. I also enjoyed the beef luau - - the meet was tender and wasn't chewy. I also tried their haupia ice cream with the warm Kulolo which is actually very tasty. I'm not a sweets guy (or I don't normally crave sweets) - but the "Sweet lady of Waihole" was good and had the same texture as bread pudding. I definitely would recommend this place! I don't know when it's not crowded - since I've only been here once, but we went on a Friday at lunch time and it wasn't too bad.
I do however - warn about car break-ins - parking alongside the road looked a little sketchy with ALL the broken glass on the ground.  Definitely keep your valuables on you and also hidden. Not everyone around showcases the good vibes of Aloha.
Live bands & casual environment. 50/50 who to avoid US military & Locals

Fun tho see food cone on an automated car.  Best to order a bowl and then add separate sushi dishes.  It's more filling at less cost.  The green tea is refilled automatically by roving workers.  The desserts are good too. Our very hot miso soup was too strong but we got it right; diluting it with some ice water.  Over all pleased. Taste of the poke was great, loco-moco gravy was good but seasoning a little strong, teriyaki beef was good. Love the signage, but prefer a place that felt cleaner with air conditioning which had less smells of the cooking from the kitchen. That may be difficult as the kitchen is not isolated from the dining area.

McDonald's in Naniakuli the order taker in the drive thru was pleasant accurate & delightful. The women at the pick up window on the other hand was unpleasant. Rude & should not have been working with the public. She informed me that each cat was only allowed 1 complimentry cup of ice/water. Since this was very different than what I experienced almost daily forever I asked for a large cup of ice & would & would pay for it  Then I asked for the Splenda & Lemon I had ordered. She handed me 1 of each. I said had ordered 4 of each. She then proceeded to lecture the women at the Pay window. Customers r only entitled to 1each! I said I still wanted them she said $,79 I asked each or for all 3? She yelled for the manager. All this while holding up the Drive Thru. About 4 minutes later s women appeared & said it would be $1.89 additional. So finally I wad so disgusted I said no thank you & left & have not been back to the Nanakuli McDonalds since! The issue is not the money it's the attitude of the workets.

She apologized, took my waffle and said she would be right back.  When she returned she actually brought me back a freshly made waffle that was nice and hot!  I was surprised! This place gets a 5 star for it's food alone. My family and I have been going their for years and even to their original location before this one and we have always favored their dim sum over anywhere else in town.

The quality of their dim sum far surpasses everyone else for it's freshness and preparation. It is always a good value here, so it won't bust your budget to splurge on a big meal there. They have all the classics and then some for dim sum.

My favorites and must haves are as follows: Spareribs, Siu Mai or Shumai aka Porkhash in Hawaii, Shrimp Dumplings, Steamed Look Fun, Chicken Feet Tripe, jellyfish, Fried Garlic Eggplant, and of course Egg Custard for dessert. Of course there is a full menu of Chinese classics you can order from if you need something more. We like to get a plate of Choy Sum with Oyster Sause for some veggies and sometimes some Crispy Fried Noodles with Seafood if feeling hungry.

Most of the staff have been there a long time and may seem a little rough around the edges, but they know what they are doing. In a a busy place like this, there is no time for niceties and it shouldn't be expected. I don't mean that they are rude, they are just not going to bend all over you like a fancy restaurant.

The place has seen a lot of traffic over the years and it shows it in the overall condition of the restaurant, but I feel that it is clean and never had any problems eating there. Don't let looks deceive you, because if you don't go based on this place's appearance, you will miss out on some really good dim sum.

You know you're in Kauai as soon as you step off the plane and you can hear the chickens. You see them as soon as you walk out of the airport as well. Super small and chill airport which I guess makes sense for Kauai. I think they have 10 gates so you cannot possibly get lost. It's also all ground floor level. They have a restaurant, a bar and even a Starbucks. It's an indoor/outdoor airport like most of in Hawaii. It definitely could use a renovation since it seems kind of dark, dated and doesn't feel clean.

Most major carriers fly here like United, Alaska, Hawaiian and American. They have car rental kiosks out front which are not open so I don't know why they're there. However the car rental places are super close, like right across the airport. You could walk to them but there are shuttles you can catch directly across the street as well in case you brought some gigantic suitcases.

Also despite it's size, don't be fooled and come right before your flight. The TSA line can back up since there's only a couple of lines and the flights all leave and depart at similar times. Make sure you get there at least an hour and a half before your flight. You don't want to miss your flight since there aren't any hotels in walking distance.  Not the best or worst airport I've been to.
