This is the real deal KFC, Korean fried chicken! I don’t eat chicken on the bone very much. And when I do, it has to be crispy and I only eat a small layer on the top. When I do eat chicken, Von’s is one of the places I go to.

They’re located on Pensacola with only metered street parking in front. We got lucky and a stall was open in front the last time we went. It’s hit or miss depending when you go. This place can get pretty busy and with the parking situation, you may want to call order in ahead. The chicken can take 10-15 minutes and that’s when they’re not busy. The owner was really nice when I asked him about the different chicken.

They have half and full orders of chicken (they’re all mixed pieces of wings and drummettes).  They have about 8 different flavors that are either oven roasted to crispy.

Half order - 10 pieces $11.99-$13.49
Full order -20 pieces $21.88-$23.49

You can also order a plate which comes with 4 pieces of chicken, rice, man doo and Mac salad. I would say stick to the half or full orders. You’re here for the chicken, remember? Forget the sides!

So what’s so great about it? The chicken is super crispy and has flavor too. I don’t like crispy chicken where all you taste is flour batter. The inside was also super moist. Crispy is my favorite! I haven’t tried the other wetter versions (remember, I have chicken issues).  We got the the crispy shoyu for a party, which is their best seller. This was a great flavor and there wasn’t a ton of sauce to make the chicken soggy. We left the box open in the bag to ensure the crispness. I was impressed, about a half hour later, the chicken was still crispy despite the time passed and having a light shoyu sauce on it. Of course, the box was almost empty too. Everyone loved it!

Stop by for a snack or when you need something for a party!

If you want something lighter or not in the mood for soup, there’s a great selection of tempura. You can select from the hot bar, or order it freshly made if you’re willing to wait a couple of minutes!

My daughter’s ramen place in Honolulu - although she prefers absolutely no toppings. The hubby and I opted for small rice dishes - grilled salmon and salmon roe, and their version of a loco moco, which included pork bits rather than a hamburger patty.

Check. This. Waterfall. Out. We were hesistent on going to Akaka Falls. As soon as we got here we were happy we made the right decision to come. Parking is 5$ (if you park inside the parking lot) and 1$ entrance fee per person. Kamaaina don't pay at all. (Completely paved, but not wheelchair accessible...if you were wondering). As soon as you walk down the first set of stairs, you have options. Short walk to waterfall (boring) or the circle route (highly recommended). The circle route takes you through lush areas where you can see smaller streams and another side waterfall then wraps around to Akaka. This is by far one of the most beautiful waterfalls I've ever seen!

Hawaiian Style Cafe is a diner. You can order anything you want. But what you should really be ordering is one of their specials. It's not listed on their menu, but on a separate board by the front.

The plate I got was ahi tuna poke and korean fried chicken over rice. I got it for takeout but even after the steam from the rice cooked a few pieces of the ahi tuna, it was still amazing. The specials seem pricey on first glance ($15+) but it's really not. You'll find that the portion sizes are huge and are actually big enough to serve two. I couldn't finish it, ate it cold the next day and it still tasted really good.

I think this might have been the best poke bowl/plate that I've ever eaten in Hawaii...or maybe even my life! If I ever find myself back on the Big Island, you can also find me at Hawaiian Style Cafe.

This lounge was super crowded. It's probably because it's the first Priority Pass lounge after security (it's also the only lounge in HNL that has alcohol available). I think we were one of the last couples to get in, and then they started turning people away. After about 10 minutes, the fire alarm went off so people started to leave. We were getting up to leave, but then it stopped. So we stayed for another 5 minutes before the fire alarm went off again. Then we left for good. Pretty suspect because the fire alarm was only in the lounge, so I wonder if someone had pulled it just to try to empty it out a bit.

The best thing was probably the cookies they had from Honolulu Cookie Company. Other than that, not much else going for them.

The Waikiki Aquarium is super small. You can definitely walk through the whole place within an hour. I guess that’s the silver lining - you won’t have to spend the entire day walking around, which is exhausting for me already, let alone for little kids. We took my niece here and my sister is a member, so not including herself, she can bring two adult guests in for free (it was me and my fiancé). Regular admission is $12, so it’s still not too bad. But the bad thing is that the parking lot is super small (15 cars maybe?), and street parking is a bit difficult to find.

It’s a nice visit if you live on the island, but if you’re a visitor, there are plenty of other things you can do if you are traveling with kids.

This another two story McDonalds.  Nice second story seating area with modern decor up a steep spiral stair case.  There is also a ADA compliant elevator.  Bathrooms had no paper towels refilled but the air blower did work. Staff was courteous  and parking was crowded.

Always passed by but never got a chance to stop by till today with some coworkers.  Ambiance is local laid back casual.  Small interior with park benches outside adjacent to open gravel paved parking area.  Peaked in to see partial kitchen view with soda machine and fridges.   Server was nice and courteous.  Food was good and most items are price from $7.50 to $12.00.  Garlic chicken was crisp and well flavored but not overly coated in sauce served with two scoops rice with either toss greens or spaghetti mac salad.  I had toased greens served with light dizzle of japanese style dressing.  Did not like the few gnats flying around but I didn't see any other pests  so that was good.  Someone got Da Elvis pancake plate and it looked good but I am not a fan of peanut butter so I passed on sampling.  Hamburger steak looked homemade with dark gravy and teo acoops rice and again eithe mac salad or greens.  In all, this is a solid lunch stop and it has ample parking for a place its size.

Pizza is great.  The Giacomo is awesome and so is the Margherita.  Make your own option is also good.  4 toppings is limit otherwise the pie gets too heavy.  Pies range from $16-$28.  Chicken wings were crunchy and tasty.  No sauce like buffalo style but good still. Onion rings were on point both being crunchy on the outside and moist on the inside even after 20 minute drive.  They were smart to poke holes in styrofoam container.  Location is dim as most bars are and beers are good.  Plenty of seats but arrive shortly after open at 11AM daily else it gets pretty crowded for lunch daily.

For the 3rd week in a row, my family and I returned to this location. The first time, I gave it a 4/5 stars. The service was wonderful. 2nd week, service was mediocre. We kept having to ask for water, napkins and put up with a server that rarely made eye contact, spoke while walking away from us, and watched workers on their phones. This week, we thought we would give it another try. NEVER AGAIN. This week, we alked in as a party of 4 but asked to be seated as a party of two. They sat us next to one another, which was great. The service was horrible. They were not attentive whatsoever. Our server from week one was working and still as awesome as ever, but only came by to drop off a platter of meat. The other two guys serving weren't on it like him. At the end of our meal, we got our checks to find that an 18% gratuity had been charged to our bill (common for groups of 8 or more). In week one, we had 6 people(2 tables), week two 5 people (2 tables), week three (2 tables/2 separate parties). Each week prior, we were not charged the 18% gratuity. However, today, EVEN THOUGH WE WERE TWO PARTIES OF TWO, we were charged an 18% tip.

Now here's the kicker! My cousin and I decide that we want to inquire about their tipping policy as the policy has changed slightly. Worker one begins to say that he isn't sure because he is new, but that because we wanted to sit at two tables, we are getting charged for a party of 8 or more. I then reminded him that when he asked us, I said there are 2 in each party. He chose to sit us next to one another. He said he wasn't sure but that he could talk to and send his manager over. The manager comes over and gives us a little but of the same response anf adds a comment about charging more for those that are bigger or need more space. This was uncalled for! We could have sat at a table altogether, but because we were paying separately and wanted to sit separately, we should have been charged separately! It should have nothing to do with how big we are! So rude. I will never go back there!

(Translated by Google) On my way back to Egg farm, where I bought my eggs,

A hamburger that a waitress aunty left me in Japanese with a shuffle of "Oh! On top of that, a piece of cooking paper is placed on the plate to prevent the oil from splashing and the hamburger is invisible.
Now, when you take the cooking paper, plenty of gravy is laid on a straw-like hamburger, and under the hamburger there is plenty of onion again, which looks delicious, but the impression is deep and heavy.

Can you get tired of the taste? Contrary to what it looks like, if you eat a bite while thinking about it, it will be very delicious when you try to eat it, and the Hawaiian food is large so I always leave a little bit, but this time my husband is surprised And one plate perori and flat.
Hamburger is highly recommended.

Now, a little bit about Dots ...
Dots is a Japanese American restaurant that opened in 1940. The inside of the restaurant is like a Showa dining room.
Waitresses worked from grandmother to grandmother, and when I entered the store, it was 3 o'clock before, but there were three loco families, about six more waitresses, three of whom were Nikkei Grandmothers.
Everyone was working with mini skirts on the knees. But after 3 o'clock, two aunts left the waitress one after another. I guess I'm probably busy at noon, because there are customers and the number of waitresses even before 3 o'clock.

Now, let ’s get back to the story,
Sizzling hamburger steak (husband is large Saimin)
If you ask for a sizzling hamburger steak, you can choose soup or salad. You can also choose bread or rice.
So, I ordered rice, but in Dotz I ordered rice and somehow the bread was first placed on the table. Of course you can eat it.
There is no additional charge and rice comes later.

In addition, McDonald's "How about potatoes together?"
Even if you do not ask for drinks, if you ask for "water", tea will also come out with the water for some reason.

I ate a hamburger like this.
I was surprised to hear that it was thymin but the taste was strong and the raw egg was on it. Because it's in the United States, there is no culture of eating raw eggs even if you eat soft-boiled.
That's why Hawaii has a limited set of eggs that can be eaten raw. Besides, the basics are that raw is NG, so I came out at a restaurant and was surprised.
However, I thought that I was using fresh eggs from a nearby egg farm as well as my home, so it was delicious.

A hamburger is a star, but it's a four star because I've hardly eaten anything else.

ウェイトレスのおばちゃんが「あっついネ！」と片言の日本語を使って置いてくれたハンバーグ。その上には油の飛び跳ねを防止する為、クッキングペーパーが皿の上に置かれハンバーグが見えない。
いざ、クッキングペーパーを取るとわらじのようなハンバーグの上にグレービーソースがたっぷりとかかり、ハンバーグの下には玉ねぎがこれまたたっぷりと敷き詰めてあり美味しそうだけど、味が濃くて重ためな印象。

味が濃くて飽きるかな？なんて思いながら一口食べたら、見た目とは裏腹に、いざ食べてみるとなんともアッサリしてて凄く美味しくって、ハワイの食事は量が多いのでいつもちょっとだけ残す私だけど、今回は旦那がビックリするぐらいバクバクと一皿ペロリと平らげちゃいました。
ハンバーグ凄くオススメです。

さて、ドッツの事を少し…
ドッツは1940年開業の日系アメリカンのレストラン。店の中はなんとも昭和の食堂的な雰囲気がプンプン。
ウェイトレスはおばちゃんからおばあちゃんまで働いていて、私が店に入った時は3時前だったけどお客さんはロコの家族連れが3組、あと6人ぐらいウェイトレスがいて、そのうち3人が日系と思われるおばあちゃん達。
全員膝上のミニスカートをはいて働いてた。でも3時過ぎたら次々と帰っていって残ったウェイトレスはおばちゃん２人。3時前でもお客さんがいる事とウェイトレスの数から、きっと昼時は忙しいんだろうなぁ。

さて、話は元に戻し今回頼んだのは
シズリングハンバーグステーキ（旦那はラージ サイミン）
シズリングハンバーグステーキを頼むとスープかサラダを選べる。また、パンかライスも選べます。
で、私はライスを注文したのだけどドッツではライスを頼んでもなぜか最初にパンが席に置かれる。もちろん食べて大丈夫👌
追加料金は発生しないし、後からライスがやって来ます。

ほかにマクドナルドの「ご一緒にポテトはいかがですか？」的なノリでドリンクとデザートも聞いてくるけど、これはもちろん追加料金発生。
特にドリンク類を頼まなくても、「お水」を頼めばお水と一緒に何故かお茶も出てきます。

そんなこんなでハンバーグを食べてきました。
そうそうサイミンだけど味が濃かったのと、生卵が乗ってたのにビックリした。アメリカだから半熟は食べても生卵食べる文化はないからねー。
だから、ハワイでは生で食べても大丈夫な卵の種類って限られてる。しかも基本は生はNGって事になってるからレストランで出てきてビックリ。
でも、我が家と同じで近くのeggファームからの新鮮な卵を使用してるんだろうなって思い美味しく頂きました。

ハンバーグだけなら星5のところだけど、他の物をほとんど食べたことがないから星4つ。

The best way to finish your "go around da island" day! No better Hawaiian food on the island unless you make it yourself. Laulau, kalua pig, lomi salmon, beef or squid luau, chicken long rice, poi, mamake tea, fresh lilikoi juice, chili pepper water...Mmmm! Sometimes they have local desserts like lilikoi, guava, or other flavors of mochi cake, cookies and breads, and ice cream. The best place to take a non-kama'aina to experience the taste of REAL, good Hawaiian food. Go hungry!!!

Since moving to the old Nihon building, there's been a good improvement. We had a mule with lilikoi juice, watermelon mojito, and some Brewhaus beers which were very good. BBQ ribs, sashimi salad and rainbow roll were great too. Sashimi salad was excellent, actually. The ice cream brownie sundae with strawberries were okay, it would have been excellent if the strawberries were fresh though. But we were feeling so good by the time dessert came so it didn't matter much. We enjoyed our time there, thanks a lot to the chef, bartenders and waitstaff. Very friendly. -- I almost forgot, the one exception. We called to make reservations and was greeted by a very sassy sounding person. As if we were bothering her by calling. Since she said there were no tables until later that night, we decided to just walk in and wait for an opening at the bar. There, the same person on the phone greeted us with the same attitude. We just let it go, smiled and took a seat to wait. There were several other groups that came in afterward without reservations to stand-by. After about 15 minutes she went to every group and nicely let them know that she'll have them seated in about 10 minutes. She walked right past us and as she did, she mumbled, "There's no openings yet." After a few minutes, she seated another couple and a small group. A couple then left from the bar, and instead of coming over to us to let us know, she stood at her counter and said very loudly and rudely, "There's an opening now if you guys want it" and waved her arm in the general direction. Now we understand that some customers can be very rude when checking availability at restaurants, as if they are owed something, so I don't blame people for the sass. But that was not us at all. Not sure what her deal was, but we didn't let it ruin our night. However, I do hope Hilo Bay Cafe reads this because with just a little more improvement, they'd be rated 5 stars.

We went in on good Friday and as it was a holiday, we had but one option on the menu: dinner all you can eat. On some level I understand it, but dont really like being forced into a dinner priced all you can eat at lunch time. The normal lunch option was the same menu with less options, however, I already commented as to how overwhelming the dinner all you can eat was. The price was something like $28 per person. Some of the meat portions were fairly skimpy and at those prices, I'd expect a decent serving size.

Frida's was highly recommended from a friend as a place to go while visiting Maui. The scene is nice, with a lot of Mexican and Frida inspired artwork and photos. I will say on a positive note, the salsa was amazing. I love that they share the recipe as well. Any refills on nachos comes at $2.50 per refill. Decent place, but I wish I had planned it as a dinner engagement rather than a light lunch spot.

This Wendys is ok. I go there fairly often. The food is good but you better check your order in the drive thru before you drive away.  From my experiences there they will mess your order up half the time. When you point out their mistake they will gladly fix it at least.

Good quality food.  Staff is very accomodating.  My vegan friend actually ordered the mapo tofu thinking it was all tofu and vegan friendly but when she got the food and saw the ground beef, she asked if she could order something else.  The waiter did so without any complaint or attitude, and he left the mapo tofu for us to eat for free, in other words, I ate it. haha.

Gotta eat the oxtail soup no matter what.  I saw the sizes that they had and it definitely wouldn't be enough for me (I can eat A LOT of oxtail soup when it's good).  Asked the waitress and she said they have a jumbo size that's not on the menu.  Ordered it, tasted amazing, I got full.  Love this place and the friendly staff
So many delicious things to try.
Great family fun spot and safe from all those nasty sharks
Killer Long island's and great burger

I tried their Confited Pork Belly Brulee! It was to tender and the brulee'd part was surprising and satisfying! The pickled onions and salad that went along with the dish worked very well with the rest of it! I will definitely be going back for more!

Went for the craftfair on Friday. Excellent crafts people were there.
Love the place! Has everything I want.
As always, the food is Ok.  Unfortunately though,  their chips and salsa is no longer for free!

Only inter island shipping company. So it does not really matter what I say cause it's your only option. What I can say is some of there employees are customer service oriented and some are not. Those that are not I think are very aware that they are the only game in town. The employees that assisted us in our last shipment were great. They really helped us to make shipping a car from oahu to Maui a fairly easy thing to do. Some just need to learn how to smile and be a little friendlier to customers as we are the ones paying there salaries.

This place is great. Just wish we had one in maui. They seem to have almost everything you need in stock. The sales people are very helpful unlike some other auto parts stores and they seem to want to go out of there way to help. Prices are great. Customer service is excellent and choices of tools and parts are both outstanding. One of my go to places when I need to fix something on one of our many vehicles.

Good burgers for a good price. I have eaten here several times and each time have tried a new burger. Prices are good for the quality and quantity of food and I have been very happy with several different  burger and fries or tater tots combos. They have sizes for everyone whether your starving or just looking for a snack. I highly recommend this place. Especially being a less expensive food option for this side of the island.

One of the last truly local restaurant. They have tons of choices. Some of my favorites are the steak bites , honey walnut shrimp, Kim Chee fried rice, but believe me there are tons of other options as well as a full bar. The staff is really good about always checking to make sure your ok. The noda chow fun is one of those dishes that you can't really find anymore and it really reminds me of my childhood days. My grandmother always used to shop at noda supermarket and she would get there chow fun with the thick noodles. This place is not only great food but it really brings back childhood memories. One of Maui's hidden gems.

Spent the day on the beach and in the bay. Dinner if the calmest water on the island with clear visibility for excellent snorkeling.

Although this theater has the most comfortable seats, and it's relatively clean, I was very disappointed to find out that the ticket counter didn't open until 11:30am, when on the web shows operating hours from 11am. There were at least 30 people lining up to buy tickets, and we all had to wait an extra 30 min than planned.  When some of us approached the staff that were inside to ask about it, that was little to none apologies,  instead they took a defense approach. Theaters everywhere are struggling nowadays to stay in business, so you would expect a better customer service from them!
I'm a regular at this restaurant. The staff is terrific and the food is good at a reasonable price. They could improve by learning how to cook rice consistently.
Wonderful as usual. My favorite place to go. Everyone there is attentive and friendly.
Went to pictures plus for a nice frame. Staff was very helpful with suggestions for the frame. Prices are reasonable. Service is terrific!
Good place to be and also were you can see what is coming up as well.
Very nice market in the mall with accommodating perks. Shop for groceries, eat, drink .... wow! It gets crowded as the day progresses especially on the weekends.
It's been years since u last visited this mall. It is rundown. The surrounding strip malls have better options and are more kept than this one
Amazing food! Beautiful location and great service! Try the cheesy garlic bread!
Very nice facility and friendly staff!
Absolutely beautiful! Peaceful and very interesting. A must visit!
Great service, excellent drinks and amazing Pho!
Excellent food, drinks, & service every time!
Love their 2 oz wine portions & amazing panna cotta!

The veg flauta was amazing! Usually come here for Taco Tuesday, but for the amount of food on this plate, I may have a second opinion.

The five star is for my two favorite things from Dots... the Sizzling Hamburger Steak and the bar.  The Sizzling Hamburger Steak always turns heads when it comes out.  So ono!  The bar is a typical low key, local bar.  They give free pupu's and during Monday night football they give away prizes!  Theres karaoke night too... Great place to hang out.

This is, hands down, the best hotel in Waikiki.  You must be either active or retired military or DoD civilian to get a room here.  I've stayed at the Hilton next door numerous times and thus place blows it out of the water in terms of price and quality.

This is a smaller version of the downtown store. It has everything you need to cook up some major Korean grinds. They also have a small Korean food counter called Waimalu Korean Takeout. Great food! Fresh Kim chee sides. Try the BBQ pork, it's awesome.
Great friendly and helpful service from all the employees from the moment I stepped into the store till I left. Keep up the awesome customer service!
Great prices for a lot of things
Easy to find what I need - I love that they tell you what isle and bay everything is.  I appreciate the lady who took the time to go up to the top shelves to get something not in stock below.
A lot of people do this hike on a weekly basis for training. Met one woman who was on her 4th of five hill repeats! Meanwhile I had to stop a dozen times!

You can tell a lot about a hispanic/latin  resturaunt by their chips and salsa/refried beans, guacamole, and their cerviche.
The salsa was fresh, spicey but lacked flavor. I ordered the surf & turf burrito. It was huge, tasted fresh, was spicey, but it also seemed to lack distinct flavor.
I wanted this to be really really good, but it was just a little above average (3.5), but I would still go back and try something else on the menu.
Parking nightmare. Booth in wrong location. Creates a hazard for pedestrians and traffic. Nearly dropped the exit gate on my car. Claimed "the computer did it."
I love this place. Good food and friendly staff.
Safeway and Ross along with banks, gas , food court and medical offices.
Mixed plate was ono! Huge portions & excellent service.
Nice Theater with comfy reclining chairs.

Good food. Only reason for 4 stars instead of 5 is because of service.  The servers seemed to have too many tables assigned  to them so it took longer for them to check on us. We had to try to get our servers attention more than once.
Great place for the whole family ❤️♥️
This place in my opinion is way over rated with not enough attractions for the price you  pay to get in
Love the deals and the point system to get cheaper gas

Nice and clean pet store with friendly staff. Prices are a really high compared to same brands offered at Target. The treats we buy were more than double the cost. They have puppy playtime on the weekends which is really nice for the dogs to socialize.

Everything on the menu is awesome.  Huge portions.  Priced right. Always busy but line moves fast.
The Mill has wonderful happy hour  specials. Great views, relaxing atmosphere.
Lots of stock and reasonably priced. Particularly liked the quick self checkouts.
Great way to spend a wonderful day in Maui. Took the Whale watching tour and I my God it was great. Whales everywhere you look. Great crew.

For a real look at the real people of Maui.
hold on to your door handles.  Parking spaces are skinny:'(

Very cheap and filling. Local Place With No Frills. If you're hungry and don't have a lot of money and you like noodles this is the place to go
Always an amazing place  turtles everywhere
Great place for coffee and food
I like this px, but many things are higher priced than local stores. The store itself, however, is well kept, we'll stocked, and has friendly staff!
I love this place! Great to just walk around and shop leisurely!
One of my favorite beaches to visit on this island! The coves keep the rough surf at bay, while allowing for a nice, quiet beach experience. Perfect for small children!

I had a coupon for a free lobster tail when ordering a steak entree (not prime rib). I would get the NY strip instead of the ribeye, if I had to order again. The entree comes with 2 sides. They have a happy hour until 7pm and if you don't have a reservation you can always sit either at the bar or at their hi-top tables. The bar/lounge area also has 4 TVs.

If you are in the mood for some nice fresh grilled fish, this is the place to go. I had grilled opa with lobster cream over garlic potatoes, yum!! If someone in your group happens to want Prime Rib, get there early (runs out quick) and you'll get a monstrous hunk of meat (1.5-2" thick with bone), good for two and then some. You'll also have a beautiful evening view of the Ko'olau Mountains overlooking the Haiku Gardens including the sunset if you get there before the end of Happy Hour (6:30).

The best thing I like about Safeway is their "$5 Friday" sales. Other than that the store can be a bit pricey. By the deli counter there's a Chinese food take out counter that lowers their $8.99 two entrees, plus rice or lo mein special to $6 on Fridays and the sushi counter has $5 specials on Fridays too. If you don't care about prices or don't have time to price shop, they have a great choice of everything.
Thursday nights are Prime Rib night for $26. A really good and thick slice of prime rib with mashed potatoes and sauteed vegetables.

Up and coming group of retail and restaurant establishments (attempting to be the local epicenter) including "Moku Kitchen," "Fish Honolulu," "Bevy," "The Boiling Crab," "Sprint," "Highway Inn" and many more. There's also a multi-level parking garage included with the entrance in Keawe St. that is minimal in cost with validation, which is handy since on-street parking is expensive and extremely hard to find.
The best wings on the island hands down, great places to get a few beers after work or on the weekends. Can get quite busy on the weekends.
The food is so good, cozy seating so may not be able to accommodate large groups.

Ideal location the atmosphere is great, they even have live mystical performance by local artists a good selection of beer, although not a fan of the coconut wireless myself but that Black Pearl is the beer for me. The food is not ideal and especially for the price, the worst Loco Moco I've ever had in my life was from here. The sauce had no flavor and it was the smallest portion of rice for a Loco Moco on the island that I know but it was the most expansive out of any Loco Moco I've ever eaten. Other than the food not a bad spot to enjoy a game or just have a drink. I heard the Nacho's were good.
Good values. Parking and traffic is horrible.

Gina's is a well known establishment in the UH college community. They serve large portions of delicious Korean BBQ at fair prices. You can choose one or two meat choices with rice and veggies or just skip the rice altogether and get a large mix of veggies. Plates are so hearty that you may not be able to finish it in one sitting! It does get quite busy around meal times so plan for a wait and to be a little packed into their store front area. There are a couple of tables and chairs inside, but I would recommend to take your plate to go. This place is a must go to stop when you're in the Kapahulu area.

I love that Honolulu makes city parks and gardens a priority. We live close to this place and have been to several events here like the art installation. The flowers and trees are beautiful. The park is a wonderful place to have a date or a picnic.

Love, always love. Best poke on the island. And they help with special requests every time. The wine and beer selection is also great and priced well, so they are your one stop shop to grab poke, fish dip, drinks and snacks.
beach has plastic and trash all over. too bad tourists dont know beforehand.
Fairly good sushi with an ayce option.

Caught a flight on Southwest to Hilo. Location of SW is in lobby 6 of Terminal 2. Their gates are on the far diamond Head side (g gates) and the only CP open is number 3.

You don't have to walk all the way to gates though as above CP4 is a wikiwiki shuttle that will take you almost all the way there. Otherwise it's about a 10 to 15 min leisurely walk.

HNL is a pretty run down airport although they did renovate some of it. The bright spot in Terminal 2 is their garden court area located just behind CP3. If you have some time to kill that's a nice place to do it.

First off they have their own parking lot that can accommodate 15 cars and that's always a plus in town. But even if that fills up there is still street parking available.

The restaurant is nicely appointed and I guess it used to be a bank previously as there is a huge vault door located by the restrooms.

They have lunch specials from 1130 to 0300. Most dishes will be in the 20 to 30 dollars with the majority on the lower half of that range.

We got the pescatore rosso and mix paella. Both dishes were full of flavor and while the rosso wasn't a huge serving it had lots of seafood in the dish.

Service was fast and friendly with a bill of about 65 with tip. Pricey for lunch but I think worth a splurge from time to time.
Food is amazing. Ube pancakes and porkbelly oinker blew my mind!
The best experience of your life
Food was awesome and the service was outstanding.

Bad service, worse management. I only received a partial order, never got my drink. There was only one server for all the tables in the restaurant. Management was not trying to help him and instead cleaned the closed bar area. This was not the first time I received bad service at this location. I am not interested in their efforts to buy a better review from me for a small gift card, but it looks like many people change their reviews on Yelp after being contacted by their social media manager. Personally I just want better service and there are a lot of other restaurants in the area.

Nice venue. Plenty of parking. Upstairs area had a lounge with Items to view. Nice staff and snack service. Had a stiff drink. Show was great. Very dirty carpets kind of made the experience a bit dingy. That’s the only knock.

No drinks in the cafe. No water fountains. Water bottle dispenser out of order. Not a way to get a drink in the whole place. How about having one employee selling the bottles? Also, the hot dog I choked down with no drink was cold.
Good Teri burger and fries. Kitchen can be slow.
Having dinner with my husband after a hard day at work makes this worthwhile and the food and service is always excellent!
Minimal effort annie's no traffic in the store
It's fun to browse in here. Always find new products, fun gadgets, and inexpensive home goods.
Lots of parking. Good mix of shopping, groceries, restaurants, etc.
Awesome food an snacks they provide. Love it
Great service love the price match.

Brings back old memories of long ago. Everything still taste the same and looks the same.

The won ton min still comes in one size with all the fixings. The char su (which I think is homemade), fresh won ton, ham and the dashi. I dashi still taste as I remember and not to salty.

I did try the BBQ chicken, it was a little on the fine side so the Teri sauce was needed. My thoughts are that they precooked the chicken sticks and just warmed them up but it was still good.

I hard Ice, reminds me of small kid days. Frozen juice in a dixi cup 🤣

I didn’t buy my car from this dealership but I do love there service department.

I called and got an appointment within 2 weeks. I showed up a little early and was surprised that they had a team of service writers ready to check people in.

One hour later as promised, my car was ready. I didn’t wait but returned at the time promised and it was completed.

Nice relaxed trail. Birds are amazing. Mountains and sea all at the same time
Nice liesure treck, lots of nature, spectacular views and a surprise from H3
Great views of waikiki, my favorite mai tai in waikiki.
Market in Chinatown with a wide variety of produce, meat, seafood and Asian specialty foods. Go to shop, or just to enjoy the marketplace atmosphere.
Great place for a mai tai. Its topped with lilikoi foam.
Very delicious gelato in classic and tropical flavors. The affogato is my favorite. Staff is very friendly!
If you like such flowers, this is the place to see them.

I'm glad this place opened back up for dine-in service. We went there this past week with a group of friends. They had reasonable precautions. A temperature check was taken when we walked into the restaurant and we were seated in a private booth. I love the quality of the meats at this place. Service was quick and friendly. Lots of parking behind the restaurant as well.
Reliable ontime and most important clean bathroom.
Clean good prices food was delicious.
Best grocery store in town! Good prices and great value.
Always a good time to be had here. If you want a great view for dinner this does not disappoint. Good drinks with souvenir glasses to boot!
Great location for shopping. They have some restaurants but hours are different because of Covid.
Amazing authentic Hawaiian food! Strongly recommended!
World class performance by local cast, fabulous stage sets in an iconic grand theater.
Pretty good service and consistently excellent food!
Grand resort hotel that has tons of features to make your stay always memorable.

Traditional style Ramen with a decent amount of options. Service was quick and courteous. Parking can be cumbersome in the parking lot directly in front of the restaurant, but there's a free parking structure across the street.
First time eating here. The food was incredible, and the servers were very nice. Only challenge was having to continuously ask for a refill on our beverages (Ice Tea).
I've never had bad experience here.
This place is pretty epic! Every unit has a washer and dryer. There are more complimentary things here than any other hotel I've stayed.. Definitely recommend.

I had high impressions of Moose.. I've been there easily over 50 times this year. I live in Kona, but am in Waikiki at least 100 times a year.. When I inquired about the VIP service I was told that it's $50 and that's VIP access for year. Honestly that's an amazing deal. But in order to get set up you need to enter, the club which cost $10. One would think that the $10 would be applied to the $50 VIP. Nope. That's not VIP service. Tell people interested in the VIP service that it's $60. Done with this spot..
This is an extremely busy store with a lot of activities going on. So busy that the staff were unable to help me find what I needed and there always is a line!
Close to the road and nice , clean and refreshing water.

My favorite place to shop. I live in Honolulu, Hawaii and the one on S King street and Ward Avenue are my favorites. I can spend up to 5, 6 hours in there and still want to come back the following day.
I shop here once a week. You'll find cheap prices on everything you can think of. Especially foods.

First time we ate here I had the french toast with berries. It was delicious, and the portion was huge! Could barely eat it all by myself. My husband ordered eggs benedict. It was perfection. The hollandaise sauce was on point. Today we had brunch there and we had the Lihing Mui sweet potato fries. I loved the sweet and salty flavors. I ordered a Pao Pork, which tasted really good and reminded me of Vietnamese sandwiches. The "bread" used is similar to a mauna pua (pork bun). It was a nice atmosphere though a little loud, but that's what happens when you eat in a popular cafe.

Hands down best Indian restaurant I’ve been on Oahu so far. Their potato stuffed naan is addictive and highly recommended. Their portions are good and prices are reasonable. I’ve been trying to find a go-to Indian place and I think this is it. The atmosphere is really nice and laid back.  Less crowded than other places we’ve been to. Weekends street parking is allowed so we just parked on the street.
I ordered the malai kofta, which was delicious! I’d say that you can taste the difference between mine and my husband’s dish. He ordered a lamb dish but I forget the exact name. We’ll definitely be back!
It was pretty good. Nice and clean.

Very busy but it was Presidents Day. Employees were nice & helpful despite the long lines.
Great poki selections and the service there is wonderful! Love his sense of humor.
Friendly staff, great food, and relatively low prices.

L&L liliha, first and oldest L&Ls here in Hawaii. I give them 4 stars because they give alot. You can also get mix plates. They let you mix any comb. Yup any... steak and beef stew, hamber steak and garlic shrimp... if you like you can mix 3... just pay little more, just saying, it's up to you... yummy

It's a nice place to go. There are many little shops and stores there. Even the surrounding areas near that area offers more shopes and stores for variety. Anything from poke and sushi to coffee, sandwich shops and Asian to western, South American eating places. The neighborhood is very pleasant and stores/shoes are nestled in cool clean area... lots of parking... go take your time walk around and enjoy the area.

My fiance and I eat at the Kailua Assaggio every so often and really enjoy it there. This is the first time dining at this particular location and it was on par with the one we're used to. You get big portions and the food was tasty. Caesar salad was excellent. Our Waitress was very friendly. The reason I give this 4 stars rather than 5 is because our waitress wasn't as attentive as we'd like. We were clearly done eating and ready for our check, but had to wait longer than we should have. But, this definitely is no deal breaker. We would dine here again. If I could give it 4½ stars, I would.

This place is fantastic for buying your food in bulk. Possibly better than Costco and Sam's. Where else can I buy a 80LB box of chicken thighs for under $1 a pound. They have your standard sizes you see in the grocery store but it's far more cost effective buying the giant sizes. Sizes far bigger than I've seen in other bulk stores. Great for stocking up your kitchen for a month or two. They even have a lot of kitchen tools and fine knives you typically only see online living in Hawaii. Word of caution though, bring a jacket. Their walk in cooler with the meats and produce is freezing. I love the cold and even I couldn't handle. They have jackets to borrow but using the same jacket as complete strangers? Ew, no thanks. But I can't recommend this place enough.

This resort has ruined all other resorts and hotels for me. This location is absolutely gorgeous and the customer service is impeccable. I was treated like a VIP by the staff from the moment I arrived to the day I checked out. From the guest rooms with huge master bathrooms to the adult only infinity pool. Everything about this place is perfect. I've never been so relaxed and content with the place I'm staying. It has all the amenities you'd come to expect from a 5 star resort and then some. I definitely hope to stay here again soon.

This theater is significantly more enjoyable since they've gone through renovations. The seats are now big cushy red recliners that allow you to lean back and put your feet up while you watch the movie, very comfortable. You can also book your tickets and seats online with an additional "convenience" fee which is cool. Try to avoid the first two rows closest to the screen: the viewing angles are worse the closer you get. I was seated in the second row and I had to keep turning my head to the left and right depending where the action was going on, made me a bit dizzy after awhile. Also, sound quality is pretty bad. My TV at home with a mid-range sound system sounds significantly better.

The workers are pleasant, and the concession stand has all the mandatory theater food fair. They're generous with the popcorn which is always appreciated!

Overall, a good place to catch the newest movies!
Took the kids and watching the excitement on there faces seeing all the animals, was great...
Friendly staff, seated right away, food arrived quickly, very delicious.

Mellow family country style
customer service.
Food came out very fast! On a busy Friday Night!! Enjoyed very much!
Picking up food for my kids. Quick, easy, close to home.
My son is Security Officer at this site. So I am here & at other information stores often!
I absolutely love their chicken wings. Best wings on the island.
Hidden path ways. beautiful hide away.
My favorite place for restaurant supplies and if you're not willing to pay for a wholesale club membership, this is a good alternative for bulk buying.

Good food! Decent prices for the portions. Upstairs restaurant, open air so no AC but you get enough breeze to be ok. Waffle fries are soooo good! We ordered the nachos which are good size and has good toppings, not enough guacamole though. My husband ordered the burger and fries and said the burger was amazing! I ordered the bowl of clam chowder and organic mixed green salad with white balsamic dressing. Everything we had was great flavor and nice portions. We were stuffed and couldn’t finish it all. Definitely worth the trip for casual American food. It’s also across the street from a beach so you can always order to go and eat on the beach.

Good place. Nice atmosphere. Near other food trucks so you can enjoy different stuff before and after if you have room! Good size burger. It’s not thick but it’s the size of the bun so it’s a good size. You can customize it how you want. We ordered the date combo with two cowboys, fries, salad and two drinks. Considering how expensive it is on the island for EVERYTHING, this was a good deal. I wish they would have a description of the sauces on the menu. We got the house sauce on the side because we’ve never been here and we weren’t sure what it was. It’s just a mixture of ketchup, mustard and Mayo. The giant onions rings were AMAZING! Super crunchy and giant. I didn’t care for the salad dressing but the salad was good! The fries were really good especially the thicker slices. The thin ones were more crunchy. We put the house sauce on the side of the fries not knowing what it was plus less messy. There is a restroom at this building so you can wash your hands after. There is a shaved ice spot right there for dessert as well. They have a few picnic tables outside so you can enjoy sitting and watching the sun go down behind the mountain.

Soooo goood!!! We ordered: cinnamon rolls, lilikoi malasada, chocolate malasada and a coconut turnover. There is covered eating areas but only two tables per covered area. A small koi pond and geckos everywhere enjoying broken frosting on the ground. It’s a tourist attraction and I can see how they grew so big. Everything was so good and I would stop everyday if we could. We stopped on our way to green sands from the volcano park. Lots of parking for this place, clean bathrooms too.
Beautiful trail and depending on what day you go, not many tourists!
Great place to get the things u need for a fast dinner or snack ....
Walmart will always be Walmart...same o'sheettt. Same boring employees of Walmart, who always seem not 2know sheettt...& it's their job 2know. Lol.

This place is a great find 4me. Went somewhere else before but they changed cooks. Their kau yuk was great diff taste from other place. Tried other foods on menu all was great. I would recommend this place.
Chicken katsu was over fried- coating burnt

Love that place. Very quite and the scenery is to die for, yet great park for family gatherings. Plenty of parking and clean restrooms. Lots of tourist at times to stop and take in the beauty of the Chinamen's Hat.

This national monument and pond in Kihei, Maui is not just peaceful but beautiful. You can walk the boardwalk and take in the pond which sits on both sides of the road. On some days, you can see birds, esp ducks and egrets, among other wild life like wild pigs.

You can also use the beach access to go to a section locals call Sugar Beach.
It's a beautiful area, the water is great, but it's also an area protected for Green Sea Turtles laying and hatching during the season.

Just remember to stay away from the animals-- its illegal to touch or interfere with them, and fines are go into the many thousands of dollars per offense. Honu's are respected in Hawaii, please be cultural ly respectful. Don't swarm them, don't get close-- even if your dying for a selfie, cute video, or "experience". If you're worried or see people harassing the animals, call the police.

I want to share my experience I just had at Hanauma Bay. I give this place 0 stars amd this is why. I just drove a family, an 8th month old pregnant mom, her 10 year old son and husband. The parking lot was full so they told me I couldnt go down. I told the security that I had a pregnant lady and she cant walk down the hill and to make her do so is absurd. They continued to not let me in but kept arguing until they did. They finally let me in and I approached the entrance with the Asian parking lady attendant with glasses. An older lady in her mid to late 50s. I am telling you this so if you see her you can give her a big middle finger. What happened next was just mind blowing. I asked if I could drop the family off and they said no, I had to take them back up and wait until the lot opened amd I can drive back down or they can walk down.  I explained she was pregnant and cant walk and that they can walk down or wait. I can't wait because I'm doing other pick ups and drop offs. Any ways. This is bothersome because for 1 these people are tourist who spend thousands of dollars to visit our beautiful state and this is how we treat the people who bring in the majority of our economy. 2nd while I am arguing with this lady, cars are leaving and there are openings but she still wont let me in but if o was a taxi driver I could go in. That makes no sense at all. So I just told the family to go and have fun, they can't arrest you or do anything and the lady was telling them they couldnt get out of the car. Since when do we start treating people this way who are who are visitors to our state. I hope the person in charge of Hanauma bay reads this because your policies and rules are retarded. Just as retarded as the lady working the front gate. You and the workers should be ashamed of yourslves for treating people this way, especially a pregnant mother. If I were a taxi driver there wouldn't be a problem. Not only does that not make any sense but just plain stupid.  And its probsbly because you're getting paid by the cab companies. Shame on you Hanauma bay for mistreating a 8th month old pregnant lady and her family.

We tried this place for the 2nd time.  The 1st time we are here was when they first opened up and we weren't impressed BUT this time, we enjoyed our foods.  The wait wasn't too bad considering it's a Saturday.  We didn't get our utensils until we asked once our main course came. They made it up with the delicious food and the drinks that they kept refilling(non-alcoholic). Appetizer deep fried sausage ravioli was great as a starter. A must try!  The Texas rub and rajun cajun ribs was delicious.  My daughters enjoyed the bulgoghi fries and sirloin.  Will definitely come back again.  Waitress was great too!

We actually made reservation online for 2.  We didn't know if they were going to be busy.  This was our first time going.  I must say, I'm actually really glad we went to try the food! She helped me decide on what to eat as well as my delicious dessert that we all tried for the first time lol. I guess they just made that flavor. We got the ribeye and the West side burger! Both cooked exactly how we like our meats. My husband is a very picky eater and he ate all his burger.  I finished my plate with the help of the husband stealing some ribeye from me lol. There was only one time when I was upset with a worker.  She worked in the craft and sewing section.  We asked for something and she didn't even look in the back if they had it.  I showed her that online it says they still had some in stock.  But she didn't want to check. She was a young girl. I ended up calling Waipahu Walmart and that lady said she didn't have any on the floor but she will look in the back.  Sure enough she said she would hold it for me.  I went at bought it.  I told her what happened and she said, the girl should've checked in the back. She must've been lazy.  Other than that, I like going to Mililani Walmart.  I've never had any issues after that one time.  It's normally clean and the staff is usually friendly. Very welcoming and even sweet when we leave.
Here is a tip for everyone: Download the Walmart app.  When you shop, scan your items to see if it's cheaper online.  You can get the online deal. The checkout employees are so helpful and they will put it in for you!!! Saved me lots of money! 🙂

This small Mexican restaurant is decent, casual, affordable, and has a cool mixture of authentic and Tex-Mex options on the menu -- I recommend it for these reasons. I love Mexican food and, for the most part, thought my food was great. The tortilla chips they make were delicious, however, the salsa that came with them was disappointing. Their homemade was watery and not spicy at all. Since they make the salsa fresh themselves, it is possible that this was a single, poorly-made batch. The burrito and fish taco tasted excellent, though the salsa served was the same one that I didn't like coupled with a salsa verde that also wasn't particularly spicy.

I would have given 5 stars if the food was a tiny bit better. It is hard to please everybody, but the mammoth burrito could have used a really spicy salsa to go with it and I do not think this would be too difficult for the kitchen to offer, especially at a place like this. Come here during lunch for delicious $4.50 tacos or for drink specials during happy hour on Tuesdays.

Cool setting, outside by the course, TV with golf, good food and drinks.
Very educational tour with breathtaking views on the top deck. My family quite enjoyed the experience and learned a lot. We highly recommend it.
Save your money from this tourist trap. Just across the street is the ocean. You're in Hawaii...

A really well priced place for decent dim sum. They cook it right! Not the best or freshest ingredients but I can't complain too much because the prices are low. The only thing I really didn't like was the beef. We'll be back!
A very beautiful beach at the end of an extremely unimproved dirt road.

Beautiful condos with best views from the cliffs overlooking the Pacific ocean. Comfortable roomy with full kitchen, two baths, wide screen TVs, and fast WiFi. Wonderful grounds overlooking the Pacific. Located among many beautiful properties and the nearby golf course.
my son only made it 45min through Black Panther but we still had a great experience
Nice place, clean, relatively quiet for Waikiki

+Good drinks.
+Above average ribs.
+Attentive service.
+Don't have to wait for your waitress/waiter to pay.

-Slow for entree to come out.
-Cheap tasting Mac and cheese.
-Fried pickles are terrible (at least they have it but nothing like how you'd get them in the South).
-Salads are more boring than a side salad at Mc.Donald's.
Great service by Justin Akao bar.

Fish Tacos were good.
Great service, and B y o b
Good service and good food, love the happy hour drink specials.
No waiting in line, quick checkouts and several new items in store plus some old favorites inventory of most items very good.

Last nite Southwest Airlines threw down a free event  at Charleys with free drinks and Lily Meola singing. The highlight for me was when Lukas Nelson (Willy's son) whose band Promise of the Real accompanied Lady Gaga and Bradley Cooper in A Star is Born as they sang that duet The Shallows.

I love Fleetwoods. First of all I never know when I'll  run into Mick Fleetwood or Jolly Ol St Mick as I like to call him. Whatta sweet guy! I enjoy sparing with him. He is so playful.
Then there is this ambience that feels like a nostalgic rockstar English pub and that cant be beat. But what tops the cale is that its multi-storied and each floor has it's own special qualities and music and menus and attracts a diverse mostly interesting crowd, typically of tourists but ya never know....
My favorite parts of the establishment are seats by the 2nd story windows th at overlook Front Street. (And on Halloweeen they are especially coveted to oversee the parade of costumes down below.
Then there is the icing on top of it- the rooftop bar. Open aired and pretty intimate sensual in atmosphere.
Overall you cannot go wrong stopping by Fleetwoods.
Since the resort was closed we snorkeled in the reefs off shore. Lovely
Went to Hyatt Hawaian Luau, it was good, but The Old Hawaian Luau, is better!
Try It,  you'll be surprised & Satisfied!
Love the location and the view!
Mars-like landscape meets beautiful tropical ocean. Just don't get too close to the blow hole or ocean. Big swells hit this side. I lost a camera from a back splash.
You need to go here for a taste of real Hawaii, a genuine malasada. Real good......
Great islands, however if you like a littering don't come here you'll probably be beat up. Hawaiians hate littering. (((Hate)))!!! littering
This is an amazing park for kids.
They're expensive but they have good stuff and they're environmentally friendly, they've also got a lot of art and stuff to sell.
Nicely organized. Crowed late afternoons and early evening. Easy to get in and out midday.
It might be the good place for football fan. It is too noisy for me
The highest price store on the island
Take the plantation tour and finish with a cocktail at one of the greatest bars on the side light! Be prepared for some odd food choices.
SO Happy we have this in Kihei now!

Lots of close parking to door, building is clean & well maintained. Has Starbucks. The priciest grocery in Kihei, but worth it for better selection on many things. Took 1 star off because I buy my produce elsewhere
Very good food, cheap happy hour drinks, mediocre service. Super noisy on latest trip. Ladies room has 1 stall, with original weird purple toilet from 2001!

Update:. So, I went back again, hoping the food was better.  NOPE!!! I immediately set in on eating the curly fries, as they're never great once cold. Well, they were already cold. I also had the bacon Jack cheeseburger.  Again, tasted like rubbery cardboard.  The only thing good was the strawberry milkshake. I won't be returning.

The food tastes like cardboard.
Coolest tree ever! Lots of people during the day.

Nice little shopping center. Laundry, food, and entertainment. Certainly a place where there is something for everyone.

Of particular note is Daddy O’s. Delicious breakfast spot. Local vibe with flavor. Dishes to please kids and adults alike. A personal favorite is the Breakfast Sandwich. Portuguese sausage burger, spam, cheese, an egg cooked to order, and a hash brown patty (I put mine on the burger). Beautiful flavor and filling too. Check it out.

The restaurant is better than the deli, I think. I will split the reviews:

Deli-its gotten better over the years. A few years ago I would have written that this is basically just cafeteria food; better than some bad college cafeterias I have been too, the same as a hospital cafeteria, worse than google cafeteria.

Its gotten better. the bread was previously like WonderBread or some slightly better equivalent, and while it has improved, I would still get a wrap or foccacia.  Eggplant mozz was pretty good.

Restaurant-call ahead to make sure they don't have live entertainment. If they do, avoid this place unless you are literally insane over the performer.

Spinach travertine salad pretty good except dressing, get something else.
Ravioli pretty good. Truffle oil VERY good, absolutely get it. Gnocchi ok. If you get pizza or salad, ask then to distribute ingredients unless you really like clumps of ingredients

Great Italian cuisine. View of charming streets of Makawao. Very romantic, upcountry atmosphere. A tad upscale by Maui standards. Good diverse menu. After 10pm live music most nights-night club atmosphere with great music. Reservations recommended but walk ins are welcome.

I have wanted to try this place for a while. They a wide variety of pastries, from sweet to savory. My test is New York cheesecake. I had a slice with a refreshing cup of cold full bodied, unsweetened black tea. I like a denser textured NY cheesecake but I would get it there again.
Beware of the chocolate almond cookies. Can't ear just one.

It was easy to find parking close to where the families had started to set up. There was a lot of open space for the kids to run around and play. Restroom was clean. Only a few trees so we had to bring tents or canopies. Water and showers were all located close to the restrooms only.
call any Apple Store in Hawaii and you get transferred to someone in Texas, Florida or Timbuktoo. So frustrating
Lots of great shops & restaurants to explore &  then stop into Diamond Ice Bar for cocktails & more food! Live music too! If you're lucky you'll catch the Maui Beach Boys! Great tunes!!

The statue of the Duke is right there front and centre at Kuhio Beach, opposite the Hyatt Regency hotel. Seeing the Duke, truly announces your arrival to Waikiki.  Definitely worth a quick visit and photo with the man who brought surfing to the World.

Love this place! Mexican food in Hawaii is very hard to come by...but this place rocks! The servings are big and the atmosphere is classic. Nice place...Gotta park in front of the shops or the parking behind the restaurant. It's always full so be there early!Sorry didnt take a pic of the menu...slot of choices to choose from!
The food was great! I didn't try the alcoholic drinks but my friends liked them. Bottom line I'd definitely go here again to eat the food and maybe try a margarita next time.
Nothing really interesting here, over priced tourist shopping.

The parking lot is usually full with a mix of locals and tourists, that says a lot. Casual, friendly, reasonable prices, good selection including gluten free. They are good anytime, but I like them for breakfast. One of the best carrot cakes I have ever had.
Interesting to see but that's about it. It's a bit of a tourist trap and I had to wait a while for people to move to get a decent view of it. Really nothing else to look at in the park.

We've been to the Olympic cafe twice in the past three days (mostly because it's very close to our timeshare). We had good lunch and an -Awesome- breakfast after church this morning. It was a little pricey and the lunch fare was just OK, but breakfast was absolutely worth it.
Great resort and spa with fantastic staff .

Breakfast at the iconic Charleys was a great way to start our road to Hana. Staff were friendly, there is parking (few places on the main road in Paia do). And the food was delish! The Loco Moco was super, Pancakes were pretty good too.
